meta_impl: select exit, "$@ should not be exited.", l, "This permanent loop"

meta_op: Define conditions for a variable to be considered a valid bound for a loop, ensuring it's used in the controlling expression and not modified within the loop.
Identify upper bound checks in loop conditions where the checked variable is compared against a valid constant or unmodified variable.
Identify lower bound checks in loop conditions where the checked variable is compared against a valid constant or unmodified variable.
Define what constitutes an increment operation on a variable, including various forms of increment expressions.
Define what constitutes a decrement operation on a variable, including various forms of decrement expressions.
Check if a statement is within the scope of a given loop.
meta_impl: predicate inScope(Loop l, Stmt s) { l.getAChild*() = s }

meta_op: Determine if a control flow path from a source variable access reaches a target node without encountering an increment operation.
Determine if a control flow path from a source variable access reaches a target node without encountering a decrement operation.
Check if a loop has a safe bound by verifying either upper/lower bound checks with proper increment/decrement paths or a constant controlling expression.
meta_impl:
predicate hasSafeBound(Loop l) {
  exists(VariableAccess bound | upperBoundCheck(l, bound) | not reachesNoInc(bound, bound))
  or
  exists(VariableAccess bound | lowerBoundCheck(l, bound) | not reachesNoDec(bound, bound))
  or
  exists(l.getControllingExpr().getValue())
}

meta_op: Select loops that either lack proper bound checks or have bound variables that aren't consistently incremented/decremented, generating appropriate diagnostic messages.
meta_impl:
from Loop loop, string msg
where
  not hasSafeBound(loop) and
  (
    not upperBoundCheck(loop, _) and
    not lowerBoundCheck(loop, _) and
    msg = "This loop does not have a fixed bound."
    or
    exists(VariableAccess bound |
      upperBoundCheck(loop, bound) and
      reachesNoInc(bound, bound) and
      msg =
        "The loop counter " + bound.getTarget().getName() +
          " is not always incremented in the loop body."
    )
    or
    exists(VariableAccess bound |
      lowerBoundCheck(loop, bound) and
      reachesNoDec(bound, bound) and
      msg =
        "The loop counter " + bound.getTarget().getName() +
          " is not always decremented in the loop body."
    )
  )

meta_op: Report the problematic loop along with the appropriate diagnostic message about its bound or counter issues.
meta_impl: select loop, msg

meta_op: Identify global variables that are only accessed within their own file.
meta_impl: forex(VariableAccess va | va.getTarget() = v | va.getFile() = v.getDefinitionLocation().getFile())

meta_op: Filter out global variables that are already declared as static.
meta_impl: not v.hasSpecifier("static")

meta_op: Ensure the variable is accessed in more than one function within the file to exclude function-scope candidates.
meta_impl: strictcount(v.getAnAccess().getEnclosingFunction()) > 1

meta_op: Exclude variables declared in header files as they are intended for external access.
meta_impl: not v.getADeclarationEntry().getFile() instanceof HeaderFile

meta_op: Select and report global variables that meet the criteria for being made static, including a descriptive message.
meta_impl:
select v,
  "The global variable " + v.getName() + " is not accessed outside of " + v.getFile().getBaseName() +
    " and could be made static."

meta_op: Identify global variables that are accessed by exactly one function.
meta_impl:
from GlobalVariable v, Function f
where
  v.getAnAccess().getEnclosingFunction() = f and
  strictcount(v.getAnAccess().getEnclosingFunction()) = 1

meta_op: Ensure all accesses to the variable are from within functions (no global/static accesses outside functions).
meta_impl: forall(VariableAccess a | a = v.getAnAccess() | exists(a.getEnclosingFunction()))

meta_op: Exclude variables declared in header files since they're meant to be accessed across multiple files.
meta_impl: not v.getADeclarationEntry().getFile() instanceof HeaderFile

meta_op: Select and format the diagnostic message for variables with too large scope.
meta_impl:
select v,
  "The variable " + v.getName() + " is only accessed in $@ and should be scoped accordingly.", f,
  f.getName()

meta_op: Define a whitelist of functions whose return values can be safely ignored, typically including functions where success/failure behavior is identical.
Identify non-void function calls that are not in the whitelist and are either in void context or have their return values stored but unused.
meta_impl:
from FunctionCall c, string msg
where
  not c.getTarget().getType() instanceof VoidType and
  not whitelist(c.getTarget()) and
  (
    c instanceof ExprInVoidContext and
    msg = "The return value of non-void function $@ is not checked."
    or
    definition(_, c.getParent()) and
    not definitionUsePair(_, c.getParent(), _) and
    msg = "$@'s return value is stored but not checked."
  )

meta_op: Report the unchecked function call with an appropriate diagnostic message indicating either direct void context or stored-but-unused return value.
meta_impl: select c, msg, c.getTarget() as f, f.getName()

meta_op: Define the control flow from a parameter to any node in the function, including the parameter's accesses and subsequent control flow nodes.
Identify the first access of a parameter in the control flow of its function.
meta_impl: VariableAccess firstAccess(Parameter p) { flow(p, result) and result = p.getAnAccess() }

meta_op: Find parameter accesses that are not guarded by any condition in the control flow.
meta_impl:
from Parameter p, VariableAccess va
where
  va = firstAccess(p) and
  not exists(Expr e | e.isCondition() | e.getAChild*() = va)

meta_op: Report unguarded parameter accesses with a warning message indicating the parameter name.
meta_impl: select va, "This use of parameter " + p.getName() + " has not been checked."

meta_op: Import assertion-related predicates and types from the standard library to identify assertion expressions in code.
meta_impl: import semmle.code.cpp.commons.Assertions

meta_op: Locate all assertion expressions in the codebase that need to be checked for non-Boolean assertions.
meta_impl: from Assertion a

meta_op: Filter assertions where the asserted expression has a pointer type, indicating a potential non-Boolean assertion.
meta_impl: where a.getAsserted().getType() instanceof PointerType

meta_op: Select and report non-Boolean assertions with their expressions and a warning message recommending Boolean tests.
meta_impl: select a.getAsserted(), "Assertions should be defined as Boolean tests."

meta_op: Identify assertions in the code that may have side effects by checking if the asserted expression is not pure.
meta_impl:
from Assertion a
where not a.getAsserted().isPure()

meta_op: Report the non-pure asserted expressions with a warning message about avoiding side effects in assertions.
meta_impl: select a.getAsserted(), "Assertions should not have side effects."

meta_op: Define a macro function call that is not nested within another macro invocation and has a function-like macro definition.
Calculate the logical length of a function by counting relevant statements and macro function calls, excluding blocks, empty statements, for-loop initializers, and macro-affected statements.
meta_impl:
int logicalLength(FunctionDeclarationEntry f) {
  result =
    count(Stmt s |
        s.getEnclosingFunction() = f.getFunction() and
        s.getFile() = f.getFile() and
        not s instanceof BlockStmt and
        not s instanceof EmptyStmt and
        not exists(ForStmt for | s = for.getInitialization()) and
        not s.isAffectedByMacro()
      ) + count(MacroFunctionCall mf | mf.getFunction() = f)
}

meta_op: Count the number of assertions within a function by checking assertion locations against the function's scope.
Identify functions that exceed the minimum logical line threshold (20) but have fewer than the required assertions (2).
meta_impl:
from FunctionDeclarationEntry f, int numAsserts, int size, int minSize
where
  minSize = 20 and
  numAsserts = assertionCount(f) and
  numAsserts < 2 and
  size = logicalLength(f) and
  size > minSize

meta_op: Report functions with insufficient assertions, including their name, logical line count, and actual assertion count.
meta_impl:
select f.getFunction(),
  "Function " + f.getName() + " has " + size + " logical lines, but only " + numAsserts +
    " assertion(s) -- minimum is 2 (for functions over " + minSize + " logical lines)."

meta_op: Identify assertions and retrieve their asserted expressions' constant values.
meta_impl:
from Assertion a, string value, string msg
where
  value = a.getAsserted().getValue()

meta_op: Determine if the assertion is always false (when value is 0) or always true (otherwise) and set the appropriate message.
meta_impl:
if value.toInt() = 0
  then msg = "This assertion is always false."
  else msg = "This assertion is always true."

meta_op: Select the asserted expression along with the diagnostic message about its constant nature.
meta_impl: select a.getAsserted(), msg

meta_op: Identify typedef types that have pointer indirection in their base type.
meta_impl:
from TypedefType t
where t.getBaseType().getPointerIndirectionLevel() > 0

meta_op: Report the identified typedef with a message indicating it hides pointer indirection.
meta_impl: select t, "The typedef " + t.getName() + " hides pointer indirection."

meta_op: Identify variables with pointer types and determine their indirection level.
meta_impl:
from Variable v, int n
where n = v.getType().(PointerType).getPointerIndirectionLevel() and n > 1

meta_op: Report variables with more than one level of pointer indirection, suggesting simplification for better readability and maintainability.
meta_impl: select v, "The variable " + v.getName() + " uses " + n + " levels of pointer indirection."

meta_op: Identify all function call expressions in the codebase.
meta_impl: from ExprCall e

meta_op: Report all function call expressions with a message indicating that function pointer calls are not permitted.
meta_impl: select e, "Calls through function pointers are not permitted."

meta_op: Filter source files that are from the source directory, have a relative path, and are not auto-generated.
meta_impl:
File f |
    f.fromSource() and exists(f.getRelativePath()) and not f instanceof AutogeneratedFile

meta_op: Calculate the sum of lines of code (excluding whitespace and comments) for the filtered source files.
meta_impl:
sum(File f |
    f.fromSource() and exists(f.getRelativePath()) and not f instanceof AutogeneratedFile
  |
    f.getMetrics().getNumberOfLinesOfCode()
  )

meta_op: Select all source files in the database that contain C/C++ code.
meta_impl: File f | f.fromSource()

meta_op: Retrieve the line count metric for each source file, excluding whitespace and comments.
meta_impl: f.getMetrics().getNumberOfLinesOfCode()

meta_op: Calculate the sum of all line counts across all source files to determine the total lines of code in the database.
meta_impl: select sum(File f | f.fromSource() | f.getMetrics().getNumberOfLinesOfCode())

meta_op: Identify parameters in function declarations that are annotated with SAL non-null annotations.
meta_impl: any(SalNotNull a).getDeclaration() = p

meta_op: Find function calls that target functions with SAL non-null annotated parameters.
meta_impl: c.getTarget() = p.getFunction()

meta_op: Match arguments to function calls with parameters that have SAL non-null annotations based on parameter index.
meta_impl: arg = c.getArgument(p.getIndex())

meta_op: Check if the argument passed to the SAL non-null annotated parameter is a null value.
meta_impl: nullValue(arg)

meta_op: Select arguments that pass null values to SAL non-null annotated parameters, along with contextual information about the call and parameter.
meta_impl:
select arg,
  "Argument (" + arg.toString() + ") for parameter $@ in call to " + c.getTarget().getName() +
    " may be null, but a SAL annotation forbids this.", p, p.getName()

meta_op: Check if a declaration entry has a specific SAL annotation by name.
Determine if a declaration entry inherits annotations from its declaration rather than defining its own.
Find declaration entries that are linked as declaration and definition pairs but have inconsistent SAL annotations.
meta_impl:
from DeclarationEntry e1, DeclarationEntry e2, string name
where
  e1.getDeclaration() = e2.getDeclaration() and
  hasAnnotation(e1, name) and
  not hasAnnotation(e2, name) and
  not name = "_Use_decl_annotations_" and
  not inheritsDeclAnnotations(e2)

meta_op: Report the inconsistent SAL annotation by highlighting the missing annotation on the definition compared to the declaration.
meta_impl:
select e2,
  "Missing SAL annotation " + name + " in " + e2.toString() + " although it is present on $@.", e1,
  e1.toString()

meta_op: Identify function calls where the target function has a SAL annotation requiring its return value to be checked.
meta_impl:
from Function f, FunctionCall call
where
  call.getTarget() = f and
  any(SalCheckReturn a).getDeclaration() = f

meta_op: Filter for function calls that occur in a void context, meaning their return values are discarded.
meta_impl: call instanceof ExprInVoidContext

meta_op: Exclude cases where the query options indicate it's acceptable to ignore the return value for this specific call.
meta_impl: not getOptions().okToIgnoreReturnValue(call)

meta_op: Report the problematic function call with a warning message indicating the SAL annotation violation.
meta_impl:
select call, "Return value of $@ discarded although a SAL annotation " + "requires inspecting it.",
  f, f.getName()

meta_op: Identify declarations that are relevant for AV Rule 40 checking, including user-defined types and inline functions.
Check if a declaration has multiple definitions by comparing definition locations.
Determine if a declaration is defined in an implementation file (CppFile or CFile).
Select declarations that violate AV Rule 40 by either having multiple definitions or being defined in implementation files, excluding member functions.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, string message
where
  relevant(d) and
  (
    hasTwoDefinitions(d) and message = " should not have several definitions."
    or
    definedInImplementationFile(d) and message = " should be defined in a header file."
  ) and
  not d instanceof MemberFunction

meta_op: Report the violation location with a message indicating the specific AV Rule 40 violation.
meta_impl: select d.getDefinitionLocation(), "AV Rule 40: " + d.getName() + message

meta_op: Define what constitutes an unencapsulated type by checking if it's an arithmetic type, enum, struct-like class, or recursively checking pointer/reference/specified base types.
Identify global variables with unencapsulated types while excluding deeply constant variables.
meta_impl:
from GlobalVariable gv
where
  unencapsulated(gv.getType()) and
  not gv.getType().isDeeplyConst()

meta_op: Report violations of AV Rule 207 by selecting unencapsulated global variables with a warning message.
meta_impl: select gv, "AV Rule 207: Unencapsulated global data will be avoided."

meta_op: Define a predicate to check if an expression occurs after initialization by ensuring it's not in a constructor or destructor.
Select memory management expressions that occur after initialization, reporting them as violations of AV Rule 206.
meta_impl:
from Expr e
where isMemoryManagementExpr(e) and occursAfterInitialization(e)
select e, "AV Rule 206: Memory management shall not occur after initialization."

meta_op: Define a type that is either a non-const char array or a non-const char pointer, which are the types that should not hold string literals.
Identify string literals that are converted to non-const string types, which could lead to undefined behavior if modified.
meta_impl:
from StringLiteral l
where l.getFullyConverted().getType().getUnderlyingType() instanceof NonConstStringType

meta_op: Report the problematic string literals with a warning about the undefined behavior of modifying them.
meta_impl:
select l,
  "A string literal must not be used as a non-const value: modifying string literals leads to undefined behavior."

meta_op: Identify octal literals in the code that originate from source files.
meta_impl:
from OctalLiteral l
where
  l.fromSource()

meta_op: Filter out octal literals that have a value of zero, as they are allowed by the rule.
meta_impl: l.getValue() != "0"

meta_op: Report any remaining octal literals (non-zero) with a diagnostic message about AV Rule 149 violation.
meta_impl: select l, "AV Rule 149: Octal constants (other than zero) shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify hexadecimal literals that are present in the source code.
meta_impl:
from HexLiteral l
where l.fromSource()

meta_op: Check if the hexadecimal literal contains lowercase letters in its textual representation.
meta_impl: l.getValueText().regexpMatch(".*[a-z].*")

meta_op: Report any hexadecimal literals with lowercase letters, indicating a violation of AV Rule 150 which requires uppercase letters in hexadecimal constants.
meta_impl: select l, "AV Rule 150: Hexadecimal constants will be represented using all uppercase letters."

meta_op: Identify source code literals that are of arithmetic type and not from macro expansions.
meta_impl:
from Literal l
where
  l.fromSource() and
  l.getType() instanceof ArithmeticType and
  not l.isInMacroExpansion()

meta_op: Exclude literals that are used in array expressions.
meta_impl: not l.getParent() instanceof ArrayExpr

meta_op: Exclude literals that are used as variable initializers.
meta_impl: not exists(Variable v | v.getInitializer().getExpr() = l)

meta_op: Exclude literals used in array assignments within constructors.
meta_impl:
not exists(Assignment a |
    a.getLValue() instanceof ArrayExpr and
    a.getRValue() = l and
    a.getControlFlowScope() instanceof Constructor
  )

meta_op: Exclude the numeric values 0 and 1 from the results.
meta_impl:
not l.getValue() = "0" and
  not l.getValue() = "1"

meta_op: Select and report literals that violate AV Rule 151, recommending the use of symbolic values instead of numeric literals.
meta_impl:
select l,
  "AV Rule 151: Numeric values in code will not be used; symbolic values will be used instead."

meta_op: Define expressions that acquire resources and their corresponding resource kinds, including file operations and memory allocations.
meta_impl:
predicate acquireExpr(Expr acquire, string kind) {
  exists(FunctionCall fc, Function f, string name |
    fc = acquire and
    f = fc.getTarget() and
    f.hasGlobalOrStdName(name) and
    (
      name = "fopen" and
      kind = "file"
      or
      name = "open" and
      kind = "file descriptor"
      or
      name = "socket" and
      kind = "file descriptor"
    )
  )
  or
  allocExpr(acquire, kind)
}

meta_op: Define expressions that release resources and their corresponding resource kinds, including file operations and memory deallocations.
meta_impl:
predicate releaseExpr(Expr release, Expr resource, string kind) {
  exists(FunctionCall fc, Function f, string name |
    fc = release and
    f = fc.getTarget() and
    f.hasGlobalOrStdName(name) and
    (
      name = "fclose" and
      resource = fc.getArgument(0) and
      kind = "file"
      or
      name = "close" and
      resource = fc.getArgument(0) and
      kind = "file descriptor"
    )
  )
  or
  exists(string releaseKind |
    freeExpr(release, resource, releaseKind) and
    (
      kind = "malloc" and
      releaseKind = "free"
      or
      kind = "new" and
      releaseKind = "delete"
      or
      kind = "new[]" and
      releaseKind = "delete[]"
    )
  )
}

meta_op: Determine if an expression releases a resource either directly or through function calls, considering pointer dereferences and global value numbering.
meta_impl:
private predicate exprReleases(Expr e, Expr released, string kind) {
  releaseExpr(e, released, kind)
  or
  exists(int arg, VariableAccess access, Function f |
    (
      e.(FunctionCall).getTarget() = f or
      e.(FunctionCall).getTarget().(MemberFunction).getAnOverridingFunction+() = f
    ) and
    access = f.getParameter(pragma[only_bind_into](arg)).getAnAccess() and
    e.(FunctionCall).getArgument(pragma[only_bind_into](arg)) = released and
    exprReleases(_,
      pragma[only_bind_into](exprOrDereference(globalValueNumber(access).getAnExpr())), kind)
  )
  or
  exists(Function f, ThisExpr innerThis |
    (
      e.(FunctionCall).getTarget() = f or
      e.(FunctionCall).getTarget().(MemberFunction).getAnOverridingFunction+() = f
    ) and
    e.(FunctionCall).getQualifier() = exprOrDereference(released) and
    innerThis.getEnclosingFunction() = f and
    exprReleases(_, pragma[only_bind_into](globalValueNumber(innerThis).getAnExpr()), kind)
  )
}

meta_op: Define a Resource class representing non-static member variables that can acquire resources, with predicates to check their context and acquisition.
Identify resources that are acquired but not released in the destructor, with file and line information for the acquisition point.
meta_impl:
predicate unreleasedResource(Resource r, Expr acquire, File f, int acquireLine) {
  r.acquisitionWithRequiredKind(acquire, _) and
  not exists(Expr releaseExpr, string kind |
    r.acquisitionWithRequiredKind(acquire, kind) and
    releaseExpr = r.getAReleaseExpr(kind) and
    r.inDestructor(releaseExpr)
  ) and
  f = acquire.getFile() and
  acquireLine = acquire.getLocation().getStartLine() and
  not exists(ExprCall exprCall |
    exprCall.getAnArgument() = r.getAnAccess() and
    r.inDestructor(exprCall)
  ) and
  forall(Class c, Destructor d |
    r.getDeclaringType().isConstructedFrom*(c) and
    d = c.getAMember() and
    not d.isCompilerGenerated() and
    not d.isDefaulted() and
    not d.isDeleted()
  |
    exists(d.getBlock())
  )
}

meta_op: Check if a resource is freed in the same method where it was acquired, indicating proper local management.
Check if a resource is potentially leaked in the same method where it was acquired, by being passed to external objects or stored in variables.
meta_impl:
predicate leakedInSameMethod(Resource r, Expr acquire) {
  unreleasedResource(r, acquire, _, _) and
  exists(Function f |
    acquire.getEnclosingFunction() = f and
    (
      exists(FunctionCall fc |
        fc.getAnArgument().getAChild*() = r.getAnAccess() and
        fc.getEnclosingFunction() = f
      )
      or
      exists(Variable v, Expr e |
        v.getAnAssignedValue() = e and
        e.getAChild*() = r.getAnAccess() and
        e.getEnclosingFunction() = f
      )
      or
      exists(FunctionCall fc |
        fc.getEnclosingFunction() = f and
        fc.getAnArgument() instanceof ThisExpr and
        (
          fc.getQualifier() = r.getAnAccess() or
          fc = acquire.getAChild*()
        )
      )
    )
  )
  or
  exists(FunctionAccess fa, string kind |
    r.acquisitionWithRequiredKind(acquire, kind) and
    fa.getTarget() = r.getAReleaseExpr(kind).getEnclosingFunction()
  )
}

meta_op: Identify resources that are released in a method other than the destructor, which may indicate improper resource management.
meta_impl:
pragma[noopt]
predicate badRelease(Resource r, Expr acquire, Function functionCallingRelease, int line) {
  unreleasedResource(r, acquire, _, _) and
  exists(Expr releaseExpr, string kind, Location releaseExprLocation, Function acquireFunction |
    r.acquisitionWithRequiredKind(acquire, kind) and
    releaseExpr = r.getAReleaseExpr(kind) and
    releaseExpr.getEnclosingFunction() = functionCallingRelease and
    functionCallingRelease.getDeclaringType() = r.getDeclaringType() and
    releaseExprLocation = releaseExpr.getLocation() and
    line = releaseExprLocation.getStartLine() and
    acquireFunction = acquire.getEnclosingFunction() and
    functionCallingRelease != acquireFunction
  )
}

meta_op: Define Qt-specific types and constructors for handling parent-child relationships in resource management.
Check if a resource is automatically released by Qt's parent-child mechanism, making manual release unnecessary.
Select resources that are acquired but not properly released, excluding cases handled by Qt or template classes, and generate appropriate warning messages.
meta_impl:
from Resource r, Expr acquire, File f, string message
where
  unreleasedResource(r, acquire, f, _) and
  not freedInSameMethod(r, acquire) and
  not leakedInSameMethod(r, acquire) and
  (
    exists(Function releaseFunction, int releaseLine |
      badRelease(r, acquire, releaseFunction, releaseLine) and
      message =
        "Resource " + r.getName() + " is acquired by class " + r.getDeclaringType().getName() +
          " but not released in the destructor. It is released from " + releaseFunction.getName() +
          " on line " + releaseLine +
          ", so this function may need to be called from the destructor."
    )
    or
    not badRelease(r, _, _, _) and
    message =
      "Resource " + r.getName() + " is acquired by class " + r.getDeclaringType().getName() +
        " but not released anywhere in this class."
  ) and
  not automaticallyReleased(acquire) and
  not r.getDeclaringType() instanceof TemplateClass
select acquire, message

meta_op: Identify member functions that are defined in source code and have a concrete implementation.
meta_impl:
MemberFunction mf
where
  mf.fromSource() and
  mf.hasDefinition()

meta_op: Exclude constructors from the analysis as they inherently modify object state.
meta_impl: not mf instanceof Constructor

meta_op: Ensure the member function does not modify any member or global variables through LValue accesses.
meta_impl:
not exists(VariableAccess va |
    va.isLValue() and
    va.getEnclosingFunction() = mf and
    (
      va.getTarget() instanceof MemberVariable or
      va.getTarget() instanceof GlobalVariable
    )
  )

meta_op: Verify that all function calls made by the member function are pure (have no side effects).
meta_impl: forall(Call c | c.getEnclosingFunction() = mf | c.isPure())

meta_op: Check that the member function is not already declared as const.
meta_impl: not mf.hasSpecifier("const")

meta_op: Flag the member function as violating AV Rule 69 and suggest adding the const qualifier.
meta_impl:
select mf,
  "AV Rule 69: A member function that does not affect the state of an object will be declared const."

meta_op: Define when a member function directly accesses a member variable for reading it (not as an lvalue in an assignment).
Recursively determine if a member function (transitively) needs a member variable through calls to other public/protected member functions.
Define when a member function directly initializes a member variable through assignment.
Recursively determine if a member function (transitively) initializes a member variable through calls to other member functions.
Determine if a constructor initializes a member variable through field initializers.
Determine if a constructor initializes a member variable through assignments or function calls within its control flow.
Combine both field initializers and control flow initialization to determine if a constructor fully initializes a member variable.
Identify member variables that are not initialized by a constructor.
Determine control flow paths in a constructor that reach a node without initializing a specific member variable.
Identify problematic constructor calls that use a variable before it's initialized.
Select elements that violate AV Rule 71 by either not initializing member variables or calling functions before initialization.
meta_impl:
from Element e, MemberVariable mv, string message
where
  doesNotInitialise(e, mv) and
  message = "Constructor does not initialize member variable " + mv.getName() + "."
  or
  badCall(_, e, mv) and
  message = "Constructor calls function using " + mv.getName() + " before it is initialized."
select e, "AV Rule 71: " + message

meta_op: Identify equality operations (== or !=) in the code.
meta_impl: from EqualityOperation e

meta_op: Find pointer-to-member types used as operands in equality operations.
meta_impl: e.getAnOperand().getType() = t and t instanceof PointerToMemberType

meta_op: Determine the class associated with the pointer-to-member type.
meta_impl: t.getClass() = c

meta_op: Check if the class has any virtual member functions.
meta_impl: c.getAMemberFunction() instanceof VirtualFunction

meta_op: Report equality operations that violate AV Rule 97.1 by having a pointer to a virtual member function as an operand.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 97.1: Neither operand of an equality operator shall be a pointer to a virtual member function."

meta_op: Check if a type is an array type or contains an array type, including through pointers or specified types, while excluding certain allowed typedefs.
meta_impl:
predicate containsArray(Type t) {
  t instanceof ArrayType
  or
  containsArray(t.(PointerType).getBaseType())
  or
  containsArray(t.(SpecifiedType).getBaseType())
  or
  containsArray(t.getUnderlyingType()) and
  not exists(TypedefType allowed | allowed = t |
    allowed.hasGlobalOrStdName("jmp_buf") or
    allowed.hasGlobalOrStdName("va_list")
  )
}

meta_op: Identify public member functions that have parameters containing array types, indicating an interface violation.
Select public member functions that violate the array-in-interface rule, excluding those declared in structs.
meta_impl:
from MemberFunction m
where
  functionApiViolation(m) and
  not m.getDeclaringType() instanceof Struct

meta_op: Report the violating member function with a message suggesting the use of container classes instead of raw arrays in interfaces.
meta_impl: select m, "Raw arrays should not be used in interfaces. A container class should be used instead."

meta_op: Identify classes that contain pointer member variables, excluding function pointers and pointer-to-member types that refer to routines.
Define trivial statements as either empty statements or return statements without expressions.
Identify classes with nontrivial destructors, defined as those containing any non-trivial statements in their destructor body.
Select classes that either have pointer members or nontrivial destructors and rely on implicit copy operations, excluding structs.
meta_impl:
from Class c
where
  (hasPointerMember(c) or hasNontrivialDestructor(c)) and
  (
    c.hasImplicitCopyAssignmentOperator() or
    c.hasImplicitCopyConstructor()
  ) and
  not c instanceof Struct

meta_op: Report the class with a warning message about AV Rule 76 violation for missing explicit copy operations.
meta_impl:
select c,
  "AV Rule 76: A copy constructor and an assignment operator shall be declared for classes that contain pointers to data items or nontrivial destructors."

meta_op: Identify classes that contain at least one virtual function.
meta_impl: exists(VirtualFunction f | f.getDeclaringType() = c)

meta_op: Find destructors in the class that are not virtual, not deleted, and not compiler-generated.
meta_impl:
exists(Destructor d |
    d.getDeclaringType() = c and
    not d.isVirtual() and
    not d.isDeleted() and
    not d.isCompilerGenerated()
  )

meta_op: Ensure the class has at least one derived class, indicating potential polymorphic usage.
meta_impl: exists(ClassDerivation d | d.getBaseClass() = c)

meta_op: Select classes that meet all criteria and emit a warning about missing virtual destructors.
meta_impl: select c, "Base classes with a virtual function must define a virtual destructor."

meta_op: Identify copy constructors that have more than one parameter and are not excluded from being considered as copy constructors in template instantiations.
meta_impl:
from CopyConstructor f
where
  f.getNumberOfParameters() > 1 and
  not f.mayNotBeCopyConstructorInInstantiation()

meta_op: Report the identified copy constructors with a warning message indicating their signature compatibility with copy constructors when default arguments are considered.
meta_impl:
select f,
  f.getName() +
    " is signature-compatible with a copy constructor when its default arguments are taken into account."

meta_op: Check if a class derives another class virtually through direct derivation or inheritance chain.
Check if a class derives another class non-virtually through direct derivation or inheritance chain.
Identify classes that have a base class which is inherited both virtually and non-virtually in their hierarchy.
meta_impl:
from Class c, Class base
where
  c.getABaseClass+() = base and
  derivesVirtual(c, base) and
  derivesNonVirtual(c, base)

meta_op: Report classes with inconsistent virtual inheritance patterns, specifying the problematic base class.
meta_impl:
select c,
  "AV Rule 89: The base class " + base.getName() + " is derived both virtual and non-virtual."

meta_op: Define type compatibility between two types, including direct equality or compatibility through base types in inheritance hierarchies.
Recursively determine the base element type of an array type, handling nested arrays until reaching a non-array base type.
Identify expressions of array type that are passed as arguments to functions where the parameter type is incompatible with the array's base element type.
meta_impl:
from Expr e, Class cl
where
  e.getType() instanceof ArrayType and
  exists(FunctionCall c, int i, Function f |
    c.getArgument(i) = e and
    c.getTarget() = f and
    exists(f.getParameter(i)) and // varargs
    baseElement(e.getType(), cl) and // only interested in arrays with classes
    not compatible(f.getParameter(i).getUnspecifiedType(), e.getUnspecifiedType())
  )

meta_op: Report violations of AV Rule 96 where arrays are being treated polymorphically through incompatible function arguments.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 96: Arrays shall not be teated polymorphically."

meta_op: Define a copy assignment operator that takes its parameter by reference and matches the declaring type.
Identify a self-equality test within the assignment operator comparing 'this' with the RHS parameter.
Detect a call to a function named 'swap' within the assignment operator.
Find a delete expression on a member variable within the assignment operator.
Determine if a class has a resource that needs management by checking for pointer-type member variables.
Select reference copy assignment operators in classes with resources that lack self-assignment checks and swap calls but contain delete expressions.
meta_impl:
from ReferenceCopyAssignmentOperator op
where
  hasResource(op.getDeclaringType()) and
  not exists(op.getASelfEqualityTest()) and
  not exists(op.getASwapCall()) and
  exists(op.getADeleteExpr())

meta_op: Report assignment operators violating AV Rule 81 by not handling self-assignment correctly.
meta_impl: select op, "AV Rule 81: The assignment operator shall handle self-assignment correctly."

meta_op: Identify diagnostics tagged as 'out_of_order_ctor_init' which represent constructor initializer lists with members in incorrect declaration order.
meta_impl:
from Diagnostic d
where d.hasTag("out_of_order_ctor_init")

meta_op: Select and report diagnostics with a message enforcing AV Rule 75 about proper initialization list ordering.
meta_impl:
select d,
  "AV Rule 75: Members of the initialization list shall be listed in the order in which they are declared in the class."

meta_op: Define pairs of opposite operators for comparison purposes.
meta_impl:
predicate oppositeOperators(string op1, string op2) {
  op1 = "operator<" and op2 = "operator>="
  or
  op1 = "operator<=" and op2 = "operator>"
  or
  op1 = "operator==" and op2 = "operator!="
  or
  oppositeOperators(op2, op1)
}

meta_op: Check if one operator is implemented as the negation of another operator by examining its syntactic form.
meta_impl:
predicate implementedAsNegationOf(Operator op1, Operator op2) {
  exists(BlockStmt b, ReturnStmt r, NotExpr n, Expr o |
    b = op1.getBlock() and
    b.getNumStmt() = 1 and
    r = b.getStmt(0) and
    n = r.getExpr() and
    o = n.getOperand() and
    (
      o instanceof LTExpr and op2.hasName("operator<")
      or
      o instanceof LEExpr and op2.hasName("operator<=")
      or
      o instanceof GTExpr and op2.hasName("operator>")
      or
      o instanceof GEExpr and op2.hasName("operator>=")
      or
      o instanceof EQExpr and op2.hasName("operator==")
      or
      o instanceof NEExpr and op2.hasName("operator!=")
      or
      o.(FunctionCall).getTarget() = op2
    )
  )
}

meta_op: Determine if a class should be checked for opposite operators, excluding template instantiations and ensuring template member functions have bodies.
Identify classes that define one operator without implementing it in terms of its opposite operator.
meta_impl:
from Class c, string op, string opp, Operator rator
where
  c.fromSource() and
  oppositeOperators(op, opp) and
  classIsCheckableFor(c, op) and
  classIsCheckableFor(c, opp) and
  rator = c.getAMember() and
  rator.hasName(op) and
  forex(Operator aRator | aRator = c.getAMember() and aRator.hasName(op) |
    not exists(Operator oprator |
      oprator = c.getAMember() and
      oprator.hasName(opp) and
      (
        implementedAsNegationOf(aRator, oprator) or
        implementedAsNegationOf(oprator, aRator)
      )
    )
  )

meta_op: Report the class with a warning about missing opposite operator implementation, including location details.
meta_impl:
select c,
  "When two operators are opposites, both should be defined and one should be defined in terms of the other. Operator "
    + op + " is declared on line " + rator.getLocation().getStartLine().toString() +
    ", but it is not defined in terms of its opposite operator " + opp + "."

meta_op: Identify assignments to non-static member variables within constructor bodies.
meta_impl:
from Assignment ass, Field mv, Constructor ctor
where
  ass.getEnclosingFunction() = ctor and
  mv.getDeclaringType() = ctor.getDeclaringType() and
  ass.getLValue().(Access).getTarget() = mv

meta_op: Exclude cases where the member variable is also initialized through the constructor's member initialization list.
meta_impl: not exists(ConstructorFieldInit cfi | cfi = ctor.getAnInitializer() | cfi.getTarget() = mv)

meta_op: Select and report constructor assignments that violate AV Rule 74 by initializing non-static members in the constructor body rather than the member initialization list.
meta_impl:
select ass,
  "AV Rule 74: Nonstatic members will be initialized through the member initialization list, not through assignment."

meta_op: Define a predicate to identify member function parameters that have default values, capturing the parameter, its initializer expression, and the default value.
Identify cases where a subclass member function overrides a superclass member function and redefines a default parameter value that differs from the inherited default value.
meta_impl:
from
  Parameter p, Parameter superP, MemberFunction subF, MemberFunction superF, int i, Expr subExpr,
  string subValue, string superValue
where
  memberParameterWithDefault(subF, i, p, subExpr, subValue) and
  subF.overrides(superF) and
  memberParameterWithDefault(superF, i, superP, _, superValue) and
  subValue != superValue

meta_op: Report the redefined default parameter with a diagnostic message that includes the parameter name, the new default value, the inherited default value, and the superclass name.
meta_impl:
select subExpr,
  "Parameter " + p.getName() + " redefines its default value to " + subValue +
    " from the inherited default value " + superValue + " (in " +
    superF.getDeclaringType().getName() +
    ").\nThe default value will be resolved statically, not by dispatch, so this can cause confusion."

meta_op: Identify function calls that are made on 'this' object, either directly or through dereferencing.
Determine if an expression refers to 'this' pointer, either directly or through function calls that return 'this'.
Determine if an expression dereferences 'this' pointer, either directly or through function calls that return dereferenced 'this'.
Check if all return statements in a function return 'this' pointer, directly or indirectly.
Check if all return statements in a function return a reference to '*this', directly or indirectly.
Identify assignment operators with incorrect return type (should return class type by reference).
meta_impl:
predicate assignOperatorWithWrongType(Operator op, string msg) {
  op.hasName("operator=") and
  exists(op.getBlock()) and
  exists(Class c |
    c = op.getDeclaringType() and
    op.getUnspecifiedType() = c and
    msg =
      "Assignment operator in class " + c.getName() + " should have return type " + c.getName() +
        "&. Otherwise a copy is created at each call."
  )
}

meta_op: Identify assignment operators that don't return a reference to '*this'.
meta_impl:
predicate assignOperatorWithWrongResult(Operator op, string msg) {
  op.hasName("operator=") and
  not returnsDereferenceThis(op) and
  exists(op.getBlock()) and
  not op.getType() instanceof VoidType and
  not assignOperatorWithWrongType(op, _) and
  msg =
    "Assignment operator in class " + op.getDeclaringType().getName() +
      " does not return a reference to *this."
}

meta_op: Select assignment operators with incorrect return type or behavior, excluding uninstantiated templates.
meta_impl:
from Operator op, string msg
where
  (
    assignOperatorWithWrongType(op, msg) or
    assignOperatorWithWrongResult(op, msg)
  ) and
  // exclude code in templates which may be incomplete
  not op.isFromUninstantiatedTemplate(_)

meta_op: Determine if two types are identical or if one is a const reference to the other.
Check if a function's first parameter may be subject to implicit conversions.
Check if a function is declared as a friend by all its parameter types.
Select friend declarations where the friend function is not subject to implicit conversions on its first parameter and is not a multi-friend function.
meta_impl:
from FriendDecl fd
where
  not implicitConvOnFirstParm(fd.getFriend()) and
  not multiFriend(fd.getFriend())

meta_op: Report the friend declaration with a message about AV Rule 70 compliance.
meta_impl:
select fd,
  "AV Rule 70: Friend declarations will only be used if the friend is unable to be a member of the class for logical or efficiency reasons."

meta_op: Check if a class has a default constructor defined either directly or inherited from a base class.
Check if a class has a copy constructor defined either directly or inherited from a base class.
Check if a class has a copy assignment operator defined either directly or inherited from a base class.
Check if a class has a destructor defined either directly or inherited from a base class.
Define a ProperClass as a Class that is not a Struct.
Identify classes that lack needed member function definitions and generate appropriate warning messages for each case.
meta_impl:
from ProperClass c, string msg
where
  not definesDefaultConstructor(c) and
  not c.hasConstructor() and
  msg =
    "AV Rule 68: class " + c.getName() +
      " does not need a default constructor and should explicitly disallow it."
  or
  not definesCopyConstructor(c) and
  msg =
    "AV Rule 68: class " + c.getName() +
      " does not need a copy constructor and should explicitly disallow it."
  or
  not definesCopyAssignmentOperator(c) and
  msg =
    "AV Rule 68: class " + c.getName() +
      " does not need a copy assignment operator and should explicitly disallow it."
  or
  not definesDestructor(c) and
  msg =
    "AV Rule 68: class " + c.getName() +
      " does not need a destructor and should explicitly disallow it."

meta_op: Select the identified classes along with their corresponding warning messages.
meta_impl: select c, msg

meta_op: Identify source-defined default constructors that are not compiler-generated.
meta_impl:
from Constructor c
where
  c.fromSource() and
  c.isDefault() and
  not c.isCompilerGenerated()

meta_op: Find member variables written by the constructor.
meta_impl:
from Constructor c, MemberVariable f
where
  f = c.getAWrittenVariable()

meta_op: Ensure all member function accesses of the variable have a corresponding if statement access.
meta_impl:
forall(MemberFunction m, VariableAccess va |
  va = f.getAnAccess() and
  m = va.getEnclosingFunction() and
  not m instanceof Constructor and
  not va.getEnclosingStmt() instanceof IfStmt
|
  exists(VariableAccess va2 | va2 = f.getAnAccess() and m = va2.getEnclosingFunction() |
    va != va2 and
    va2.getEnclosingStmt() instanceof IfStmt
  )
)

meta_op: Exclude cases where the declaring type is used in array types or array new expressions.
meta_impl:
not (
  exists(ArrayType at | at.getBaseType+() = c.getDeclaringType())
  or
  exists(NewArrayExpr nae | nae.getType().(ArrayType).getBaseType+() = c.getDeclaringType())
)

meta_op: Exclude cases where the declaring type is a virtual base class.
Select and report constructors with potentially unusable state initialization.
meta_impl:
select c,
  "This default constructor possibly doesn't put the object in a usable state, indicated by the member variable $@.",
  f, f.toString()

meta_op: Define when a class directly derives from another class with virtual inheritance.
Define transitive virtual inheritance by checking if a class or any of its base classes virtually derive from a given base class.
Identify classes that transitively inherit from a virtual base class but do not directly declare the virtual inheritance.
meta_impl:
from Class c, Class base
where
  c.getABaseClass+() = base and
  derivesVirtualStar(c, base) and
  not derivesVirtual(c, base)

meta_op: Report classes violating AV Rule 88.1 by not explicitly declaring virtual inheritance from a stateful virtual base class they access.
meta_impl: select c, "AV Rule 88.1: The virtual base " + base.getName() + " shall be explicitly declared."

meta_op: Identify pairs of distinct member functions that are not virtual.
meta_impl:
from MemberFunction f1, MemberFunction f2
where
  f1 != f2 and
  not f1 instanceof VirtualFunction and
  not f2 instanceof VirtualFunction

meta_op: Ensure one function is declared in a base class of the other function's declaring type.
meta_impl: f1.getDeclaringType().getABaseClass+() = f2.getDeclaringType()

meta_op: Match functions by name and ensure their parameter types are identical.
meta_impl:
f1.getName() = f2.getName() and
  forall(Parameter p1, Parameter p2, int i | f1.getParameter(i) = p1 and f2.getParameter(i) = p2 |
    p1.getType() = p2.getType()
  )

meta_op: Select the redefined function and emit a warning about violating AV Rule 94.
meta_impl: select f1, "AV Rule 94: An inherited nonvirtual function shall not be redefined in a derived class."

meta_op: Define an interface class as a class with pure virtual functions and fewer than 3 member variables, excluding compiler-generated elements.
Define a class that implements at least one interface, serving as the base for checking multiple inheritance discipline.
Count the number of interface implementations inherited by a class.
Count the number of protected non-interface implementations inherited by a class.
Count the number of private non-interface implementations inherited by a class.
Count the number of public non-interface implementations inherited by a class.
Identify classes with undisciplined multiple inheritance patterns (public implementations or more than one protected implementation).
meta_impl:
from InterfaceImplementor d
where
  d.getNumPublicImplementations() > 0 or
  d.getNumProtectedImplementations() > 1

meta_op: Report the undisciplined multiple inheritance with detailed counts of interface and implementation types.
meta_impl:
select d,
  "Multiple inheritance should not be used with " + d.getNumInterfaces().toString() +
    " interfaces, " + d.getNumPrivateImplementations().toString() + " private implementations, " +
    d.getNumProtectedImplementations().toString() + " protected implementations, and " +
    d.getNumPublicImplementations().toString() + " public implementations."

meta_op: Identify function calls that are made through 'this' pointer, either directly or through dereference.
Identify virtual function calls made through 'this' pointer and determine their overriding function.
Define non-virtual member functions of a class, excluding constructors and destructors.
Identify calls from non-virtual member functions to other member functions of the same class through 'this' pointer.
Recursively determine if a member function indirectly calls a virtual function through a chain of non-virtual member functions.
meta_impl:
pragma[noopt]
predicate indirectlyCallsVirtualFunction(MemberFunction caller, Function target, Class c) {
  exists(FunctionCall fc |
    virtualThisCall(fc, _) and
    fc.getEnclosingFunction() = caller and
    fc.getTarget() = target and
    nonVirtualMember(caller, c)
  )
  or
  exists(MemberFunction mid |
    indirectlyCallsVirtualFunction(mid, target, c) and
    callFromNonVirtual(caller, c, mid)
  )
}

meta_op: Select virtual function calls from constructors or destructors, including both direct calls and indirect calls through non-virtual functions, and generate appropriate warning messages.
meta_impl:
from FunctionCall call, string explanation, Function virtFunction, Function overridingFunction
where
  (
    call.getEnclosingFunction() instanceof Constructor or
    call.getEnclosingFunction() instanceof Destructor
  ) and
  (
    (
      virtualThisCall(call, overridingFunction) and
      explanation =
        "Call to virtual function $@ which is overridden in $@. If you intend to statically call this virtual function, it should be qualified with "
          + virtFunction.getDeclaringType().toString() + "::."
    ) and
    virtFunction = call.getTarget() and
    overridingFunction.getDeclaringType().getABaseClass+() =
      call.getEnclosingFunction().getDeclaringType()
    or
    exists(VirtualFunction target |
      thisCall(call) and indirectlyCallsVirtualFunction(call.getTarget(), target, _)
    |
      explanation =
        "Call to function " + call.getTarget().getName() +
          " that calls virtual function $@ (overridden in $@)." and
      virtFunction = target and
      overridingFunction = target.getAnOverridingFunction() and
      overridingFunction.getDeclaringType().getABaseClass+() =
        call.getEnclosingFunction().getDeclaringType()
    )
  )
select call, explanation, virtFunction, virtFunction.getName(), overridingFunction,
  overridingFunction.getDeclaringType().getName()

meta_op: Define a predicate to check if a type declaration entry is allowed by being a typedef for a function pointer type.
Identify function pointer type uses that are not covered by allowed typedef declarations.
meta_impl:
from FunctionPointerType t, Locatable l
where
  t.getATypeNameUse() = l and
  not allowed(l, t)

meta_op: Report locations where function pointers are used without typedefs, suggesting the use of typedefs for better readability.
meta_impl: select l, "AV Rule 176: A typedef will be used when declaring function pointers."

meta_op: Define a class representing pointer expressions involved in comparison operations that have pointer values.
Define conditions under which two pointer expressions may be compared, including same type/points-to relationships or null pointer comparisons.
meta_impl:
predicate mayBeCompared(PointerInComparison p, PointerInComparison q) {
  p.getUnderlyingType() = q.getUnderlyingType() and
  p.pointsTo() = q.pointsTo()
  or
  p.getValue() = "0"
  or
  q.getValue() = "0"
}

meta_op: Identify pairs of pointer expressions in the same comparison that violate the comparison rules (different types/points-to and neither is null).
meta_impl:
from PointerInComparison p, PointerInComparison q
where
  p.getComparison() = q.getComparison() and
  not mayBeCompared(p, q)

meta_op: Report the comparison operation that violates AV Rule 171 with a diagnostic message about improper pointer comparisons.
meta_impl:
select p.getComparison(),
  "AV Rule 171: Relational operators shall not be applied to pointer types except in very specific circumstances."

meta_op: Identify pointer types with more than two levels of indirection.
meta_impl:
from PointerType t
where t.getPointerIndirectionLevel() > 2

meta_op: Associate the identified pointer types with their corresponding type name uses.
meta_impl:
from PointerType t, Element e
where t.getATypeNameUse() = e

meta_op: Filter and select elements that use pointer types with excessive indirection levels.
meta_impl:
from PointerType t, Element e
where
  t.getPointerIndirectionLevel() > 2 and
  t.getATypeNameUse() = e

meta_op: Report the violation with a message indicating the AV Rule 170 violation for excessive pointer indirection.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 170: More than two levels of pointer indirection shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify source-level assignments where the left-hand side is a variable access and the right-hand side is an address-of expression.
meta_impl:
from Assignment a, Variable global, Variable local
where
  a.fromSource() and
  global.getAnAccess() = a.getLValue() and
  local.getAnAccess() = a.getRValue().(AddressOfExpr).getOperand()

meta_op: Ensure the variable whose address is taken has automatic storage duration.
meta_impl: local.hasSpecifier("auto")

meta_op: Check that the target variable either is not local, is in an enclosing scope, or has static storage duration.
meta_impl:
(
    not global instanceof LocalVariable or
    global.getParentScope() = local.getParentScope().getParentScope+() or
    global.hasSpecifier("static")
  )

meta_op: Select and report assignments that may cause dangling references by storing addresses of automatic variables in longer-lived objects.
meta_impl:
select a,
  "AV Rule 173: The address of an object with automatic storage shall not be assigned to another object that may persist after the first object has ceased to exist."

meta_op: Identify all occurrences of NULL in the codebase.
meta_impl: from NULL null

meta_op: Check if NULL is used as a right-hand value in an assignment operation.
meta_impl: exists(Assignment a | null = a.getRValue())

meta_op: Check if NULL is used as an operand in a comparison operation.
meta_impl: exists(ComparisonOperation op | null = op.getAnOperand())

meta_op: Select and report all NULL usages that violate AV Rule 175, recommending the use of plain 0 instead.
meta_impl: select null, "A pointer shall not be compared to NULL or be assigned NULL; use plain 0 instead."

meta_op: Determine if an expression is a pure operation by checking if it's an operation but not an assignment or increment/decrement.
Find the smallest pure operation expression containing the given expression where all other subexpressions are constants.
Check if an expression occurs by itself as a statement or as a for-loop update expression.
Check if an expression is on the right side of an assignment or in an initializer.
Check if an expression is used as a controlling expression in loops, if statements, or switch statements.
meta_impl: predicate isControllingExpr(Expr e) { exists(ControlStructure c | c.getControllingExpr() = e) }

meta_op: Check if an expression is the only impure argument in a function call with all other arguments being pure.
Check if an expression is part of a chain of qualifiers (either as qualifier or qualified expression).
meta_impl: predicate isPartOfChain(Expr e) { qualifies(e, _) or qualifies(_, e) }

meta_op: Define the qualification relationship between two expressions (either as call qualifier or variable access qualifier).
Identify impure expressions in disallowed contexts by checking all valid contexts and their negations.
meta_impl:
predicate impureExprInDisallowedContext(Expr e) {
  exists(Expr f |
    e.fromSource() and
    not e.isPure() and
    stripOffConstants(e, f) and
    not occursByItself(f) and
    not isOnRightOfAssignment(f) and
    not isControllingExpr(f) and
    not isSoleNonConstFunArg(f) and
    not isPartOfChain(f)
  )
}

meta_op: Select impure expressions in disallowed contexts while filtering out compiler-generated code, conversions, and certain constructor/destructor cases.
meta_impl:
from Expr e
where
  impureExprInDisallowedContext(e) and
  not e.isCompilerGenerated() and
  not e instanceof Conversion and
  not exists(@ctorinit ci | e = mkElement(ci)) and
  not exists(@dtordestruct dd | e = mkElement(dd)) and
  not impureExprInDisallowedContext(e.getParent+())

meta_op: Report the root cause of the impure expression violation with the AV Rule 204 warning message.
meta_impl:
select e.findRootCause(),
  "AV Rule 204: A single operation with side-effects shall only be used in certain contexts."

meta_op: Define the conditions under which a volatile specifier is considered acceptable, specifically for external global variables.
Identify variables with volatile specifiers that do not meet the acceptable criteria.
meta_impl:
from Variable v
where
  v.getType().hasSpecifier("volatile") and
  not acceptableVolatile(v)

meta_op: Report variables with inappropriate volatile usage, providing a warning message about AV Rule 205 compliance.
meta_impl:
select v,
  "AV Rule 205: The volatile keyword shall not be used unless directly interfacing with hardware."

meta_op: Identify equality operations in the source code that involve floating-point operands.
meta_impl:
from EqualityOperation e
where
  e.fromSource() and
  e.getAnOperand().getType() instanceof FloatingPointType

meta_op: Report each identified floating-point equality operation with a diagnostic message about the violation of AV Rule 202.
meta_impl:
select e,
  "AV Rule 202: Floating point variables shall not be tested for exact equality or inequality."

meta_op: Define a class for variable accesses that are involved in side effects, specifically those used in increment/decrement operations or as lvalues in assignments.
Recursively determine the characteristic sequence point expression by walking up the AST until reaching a non-sequence-point operator (logical, comma, conditional).
meta_impl:
predicate characteristicSequencePointExpr(Expr e, Expr c) {
  if
    exists(Expr p |
      p = e.getParent() and
      not p instanceof BinaryLogicalOperation and
      not p instanceof CommaExpr and
      not p instanceof ConditionalExpr
    )
  then characteristicSequencePointExpr(e.getParent(), c)
  else e = c
}

meta_op: Identify expressions where the same variable is accessed with side effects and regular accesses within the same sequence point, excluding direct lvalue assignments.
meta_impl:
from SideEffectVariableExpr e, Expr c, Variable v, VariableAccess va
where
  e.getTarget() = v and
  va.getTarget() = v and
  characteristicSequencePointExpr(e, c) and
  characteristicSequencePointExpr(va, c) and
  va != e and
  not e.getParent().(AssignExpr).getLValue() = e

meta_op: Report the sequence point expression that violates AV Rule 204.1 by containing potentially order-dependent evaluations of the same variable.
meta_impl:
select c,
  "AV Rule 204.1: The value of an expression shall be the same under any order of evaluation that the standard permits."

meta_op: Define a class for identifying the first comment in a file, ensuring no other relevant elements appear before it.
Define validity criteria for the first comment, including minimum length and file name inclusion.
Specify which types of elements should not appear before the first comment in a file.
Check for source files that either lack a first comment or have an invalid one, preparing blame and message accordingly.
meta_impl:
from File f, Element blame, string message
where
  f.fromSource() and
  (
    not exists(FirstComment comment | comment.getFile() = f) and
    blame = f and
    message = ""
    or
    exists(FirstComment comment |
      comment.getFile() = f and not comment.isValid() and blame = comment
    ) and
    message = "The introductory comment does not match the required style rules."
  )

meta_op: Report violations of AV Rule 133, indicating missing or non-compliant introductory comments with appropriate blame and message.
meta_impl:
select blame,
  "AV Rule 133: every source file will be documented with an introductory comment. " + message

meta_op: Identify source code comments that are not valid C++ style comments (i.e., do not start with //).
meta_impl:
from Comment c
where
  c.fromSource() and
  not c.getContents().regexpMatch("\\s*//.*")

meta_op: Report each invalid comment with a message indicating it violates AV Rule 126 about using only valid C++ style comments.
meta_impl: select c, "AV Rule 126: Only valid C++ style comments shall be used."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query for defects related to external data that matches the specific query path for AV Rule 127.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.14 Comments/AV Rule 127.ql"

meta_op: Select the identified defects along with their associated messages for reporting.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Identify switch statements where every non-default case dispatches on an integer constant.
meta_impl:
from SwitchStmt s
where
  forex(SwitchCase sc | sc = s.getASwitchCase() and not sc instanceof DefaultCase |
    sc.getExpr().(VariableAccess).getTarget().isConst()
  )

meta_op: Exclude switch statements that operate on character types from the analysis.
meta_impl: not s.getExpr().getUnspecifiedType() instanceof CharType

meta_op: Report switch statements that should use enumeration types instead of integers for limited choices, providing a maintainability warning.
meta_impl:
select s,
  "Enumeration types should be used instead of integers to select from a limited series of choices."

meta_op: Define a general pointer type that includes both pointer types and array types.
Identify invalid cast expressions where a float pointer type is cast to a different pointer type, exposing float bit representation.
Identify unions that contain both float and non-float members, which expose float bit representation.
Select elements that either involve invalid float pointer casts or are float members in mixed-type unions, with appropriate error messages.
meta_impl:
from Element e, string message
where
  e instanceof InvalidFloatCastExpr and
  message =
    "Casting a float pointer to another pointer type exposes the bit representation of the float, leading to unportable code."
  or
  exists(FloatUnion fu | e = fu.getAFloatMember()) and
  message =
    "Defining a union with a float member exposes the bit representation of the float, leading to unportable code."

meta_op: Report the identified issues with appropriate diagnostic messages about float bit representation exposure.
meta_impl: select e, message

meta_op: Define a predicate to identify potential self-modifying code by detecting conversions between function pointers and non-function, non-void pointers.
meta_impl:
predicate maybeSmcConversion(Type t1, Type t2) {
  t1 instanceof FunctionPointerType and
  t2 instanceof PointerType and
  not t2 instanceof FunctionPointerType and
  not t2 instanceof VoidPointerType
  or
  maybeSmcConversion(t2, t1)
}
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 2.ql

meta_op: Select expressions from source code that involve potentially dangerous type conversions indicating self-modifying code.
meta_impl:
from Expr e
where
  e.fromSource() and
  maybeSmcConversion(e.getUnderlyingType(), e.getActualType())
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 2.ql

meta_op: Report expressions that violate AV Rule 2 by potentially containing self-modifying code.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 2: There shall not be any self-modifying code."
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 2.ql

meta_op: Retrieve the number of logical source lines of code for each function.
meta_impl:
from Function f, int n
where
  n = f.getMetrics().getNumberOfLinesOfCode()
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 1.ql

meta_op: Filter functions that exceed the 200-line threshold for logical source lines of code.
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 1.ql

meta_op: Report functions violating the line count rule with a descriptive message including the function name and line count.
meta_impl:
select f,
  "AV Rule 1: any one function (or method) will contain no more than 200 logical source lines of code. Function '"
    + f.toString() + "' contains " + n.toString() + " lines of code."
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 1.ql

meta_op: Retrieve the cyclomatic complexity metric for each function in the codebase.
meta_impl:
from Function f, int c
where
  c = f.getMetrics().getCyclomaticComplexity()
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 3.ql

meta_op: Filter functions to only those with cyclomatic complexity exceeding the threshold of 20.
meta_impl:
from Function f, int c
where
  c = f.getMetrics().getCyclomaticComplexity() and
  c > 20
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 3.ql

meta_op: Report functions that violate the cyclomatic complexity rule with a descriptive message.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 3: All functions shall have a cyclomatic complexity number of 20 or less."
reference_path: jsf/3.02 Code Size and Complexity/AV Rule 3.ql

meta_op: Define a predicate to check if an expression is non-negative, either by being of unsigned type or having a non-negative constant value.
Define a predicate to check if two expressions refer to the same variable, focusing on variable access targets.
Define a class to identify unreliable overflow tests using the "a + b < a" idiom, checking for matching operands and non-negative terms.
Select all instances of unreliable overflow tests and report them as violations of AV Rule 212.
meta_impl:
from UnreliableOverflowTest t
select t, "AV Rule 212: Underflow or overflow functioning shall not be depended on."

meta_op: Define a type representing pointer or array types to simplify subsequent checks.
Identify casts between pointers/arrays of different integral types that expose memory representation assumptions.
Identify unions containing both an integral and an array of smaller integrals that could expose memory representation assumptions.
Select elements that violate AV Rule 210 either through problematic casts or unions, with appropriate diagnostic messages.
meta_impl:
from Element e, string message
where
  e instanceof ExposingIntegralCastExpr and
  message = "AV Rule 210: This cast makes assumptions concerning data representation in memory."
  or
  e instanceof ExposingIntegralUnion and
  message = "AV Rule 210: This union may make assumptions concerning data representation in memory."

meta_op: Report the violating elements along with their specific diagnostic messages.
meta_impl: select e, message

meta_op: Identify all pointer arithmetic operations in the source code.
meta_impl: from PointerArithmeticOperation pao

meta_op: Filter pointer arithmetic operations to only those that originate from source code (not library or generated code).
meta_impl: where pao.fromSource()

meta_op: Report each pointer arithmetic operation with a warning message about AV Rule 215 violation.
meta_impl: select pao, "AV Rule 215: Pointer arithmetic will not be used."

meta_op: Identify uses of basic arithmetic types (int, short, long, float, double) in the code.
meta_impl:
from Element u, ArithmeticType at
where
  at.hasName(["int", "short", "long", "float", "double"]) and
  u = at.getATypeNameUse() and
  not at instanceof WideCharType

meta_op: Report each usage of basic arithmetic types with a message recommending the use of specific-length typedef equivalents instead.
meta_impl: select u, "AV Rule 209: The basic types of int, short, long, float and double shall not be used."

meta_op: Define precedence levels for arithmetic operators (multiplication/division and addition/subtraction).
meta_impl: predicate arithmeticPrecedence(int p) { p = 12 or p = 13 }

meta_op: Define precedence levels for comparison operators (relational and equality operators).
meta_impl: predicate comparisonPrecedence(int p) { p = 9 or p = 10 }

meta_op: Identify parent-child expression pairs where the child has higher precedence than the parent.
meta_impl:
from Expr e1, Expr e2, int p1, int p2
where
  e1.getAChild() = e2 and
  p1 = e1.getPrecedence() and
  p2 = e2.getPrecedence() and
  p1 < p2

meta_op: Filter out cases where the child expression is parenthesized (explicit precedence).
meta_impl: not e2.isParenthesised()

meta_op: Limit the scope to operators with precedence at or below arithmetic operators (precedence <= 13).
Exclude cases where arithmetic operators are mixed with comparison operators (allowed deviation).
meta_impl: not (arithmeticPrecedence(p2) and comparisonPrecedence(p1))

meta_op: Exclude cases where the child is the right-hand side of an assignment (allowed deviation).
meta_impl: not e2 = e1.(Assignment).getRValue()

meta_op: Report violations of AV Rule 213 where operator precedence rules are overly relied upon.
meta_impl: select e1, "AV Rule 213: Limited dependence shall be placed on operator precedence rules."

meta_op: Determine if an expression is part of a global variable initializer in a specific file.
Check if a function is called from within a global initializer in a specific file.
Determine if an expression is evaluated before the main function, either directly in a global initializer or within a function called from one.
Check if two files belong to the same translation unit by checking inclusion relationships.
Identify variable accesses that occur before main in one file but access a variable from a different translation unit.
meta_impl:
from VariableAccess v, File f1, File f2
where
  v.fromSource() and
  v.isRValue() and
  evaluatedBeforeMain(v, f1) and
  v.getTarget().getFile() = f2 and
  not sameTranslationUnit(f1, f2)

meta_op: Report violations of AV Rule 214 where initialization order assumptions are made across translation units.
meta_impl:
select v,
  "AV Rule 214: It shall not be assumed that objects in separated translation units are initialized in a special order."

meta_op: Define a predicate to exclude certain expressions (like literals) from analysis where signed/unsigned distinction is less meaningful.
Define a predicate to identify unsigned operands by checking their underlying type and ensuring they are not excluded expressions.
Select binary arithmetic operations where both operands are unsigned, as identified by the isUnsignedOperand predicate.
meta_impl:
from BinaryOperation op
where
  op instanceof BinaryArithmeticOperation and
  isUnsignedOperand(op.getChild(0)) and
  isUnsignedOperand(op.getChild(1))

meta_op: Report the binary operation with a message indicating violation of AV Rule 163 regarding unsigned arithmetic.
meta_impl: select op, "AV Rule 163: unsigned arithmetic shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify binary logical operations (&& or ||) that are directly from source code.
meta_impl:
from BinaryLogicalOperation blo
where
  blo.fromSource()

meta_op: Find binary operations that are operands of the identified binary logical operations.
meta_impl:
from BinaryLogicalOperation blo, BinaryOperation bo
where
  bo = blo.getAnOperand()

meta_op: Filter out binary operations that are properly parenthesized, leaving only those that violate the parenthesization requirement.
meta_impl:
from BinaryLogicalOperation blo, BinaryOperation bo
where
  not bo.isParenthesised()

meta_op: Select and report the binary logical operations with unparenthesized binary operands, providing a diagnostic message about the violation.
meta_impl:
select blo,
  "AV Rule 158: The operands of a logical && or || operator shall be parenthesized if the operands contain binary operators."

meta_op: Identify functions that overload the logical AND operator (&&) and generate a warning message about losing short-circuit semantics.
meta_impl:
from Function o, string message
where
  o.getName() = "operator&&" and
  message =
    "AV Rule 159: the && operator shall not be overloaded as short-circuit semantics cannot be obtained."

meta_op: Identify functions that overload the logical OR operator (||) and generate a warning message about losing short-circuit semantics.
meta_impl:
from Function o, string message
where
  o.getName() = "operator||" and
  message =
    "AV Rule 159: the || operator shall not be overloaded as short-circuit semantics cannot be obtained."

meta_op: Identify functions that overload the unary address-of operator (&) and generate a warning message about potential undefined behavior.
meta_impl:
from Function o, string message
where
  o.getName() = "operator&" and
  o.getNumberOfParameters() = 1 and
  message =
    "AV Rule 159: the unary & operator shall not be overloaded because of undefined behavior."

meta_op: Select all identified operator functions with their corresponding warning messages for reporting.
meta_impl: select o, message

meta_op: Define when a variable has a possible value from either its initializer or subsequent assignments.
Determine if an expression has a constant integer value, including through variable accesses where all possible values are constant.
Identify shift operations where the right operand's constant value violates the rule by being outside the valid range for the left operand's bit width.
Select shift operations with violating right operands and compute the minimal violating value for reporting.
meta_impl:
from BinaryBitwiseOperation op, int lhsBytes, int canonicalValue
where canonicalValue = min(int v | violation(op, lhsBytes, v))

meta_op: Report the violating shift operation with a diagnostic message showing the invalid value and valid range.
meta_impl:
select op,
  "AV Rule 164: The right-hand operand (here a value is " + canonicalValue.toString() +
    ") of this shift shall lie between 0 and " + (lhsBytes * 8 - 1).toString() + "."

meta_op: Identify binary logical operations (&& or ||) in source code that have right-hand operands with side effects.
meta_impl:
from BinaryLogicalOperation blo
where
  blo.fromSource() and
  not blo.getRightOperand().isPure()

meta_op: Report the binary logical operation with a warning about side effects in the right-hand operand, following AV Rule 157.
meta_impl:
select blo,
  "AV Rule 157: The right hand operand of a && or || operator shall not contain side effects."

meta_op: Identify instances where the sizeof operator is applied to expressions that may have side effects.
meta_impl: from SizeofImpureExprOperator sz

meta_op: Report each problematic sizeof operator usage with a warning message about potential side effects.
meta_impl:
select sz,
  "A sizeof operator should not be used on expressions that contain side effects as the effect is confusing."

meta_op: Define a predicate to identify expressions that are either direct assignment expressions or nested within comma expressions.
Define a class to represent expressions that contain multiple assignments, either directly or through nested comma expressions.
Define a class to identify expressions used as side effects in for statements, either in the update clause or as part of the initialization.
Select assignment expressions that violate AV Rule 160 by not being used as the sole expression in an expression statement and not being part of a for statement side effect.
meta_impl:
from AssignExpr ae
where
  ae.fromSource() and
  not ae.getParent() instanceof ExprStmt and
  not ae instanceof ForStmtSideEffectExpr

meta_op: Report the violating assignment expressions with a diagnostic message indicating the AV Rule 160 violation.
meta_impl:
select ae,
  "AV Rule 160: An assignment expression shall be used only as the expression in an expression statement."

meta_op: Exclude certain expressions (like literals) from analysis where signed/unsigned distinction is less meaningful.
Identify expressions that are explicitly converted to signed integral types, excluding the excluded expressions.
Identify expressions that are explicitly converted to unsigned integral types, excluding the excluded expressions.
Select binary operations (arithmetic or comparison) that mix signed and unsigned operands.
meta_impl:
from BinaryOperation op
where
  (op instanceof BinaryArithmeticOperation or op instanceof ComparisonOperation) and
  isSignedOperand(op.getAnOperand()) and
  isUnsignedOperand(op.getAnOperand())

meta_op: Report the binary operation with a warning about mixing signed and unsigned values according to AV Rule 162.
meta_impl:
select op,
  "AV Rule 162: signed and unsigned values shall not be mixed in arithmetic or comparison operations."

meta_op: Identify all unary minus expressions in the code.
meta_impl: from UnaryMinusExpr ume

meta_op: Check if the operand of the unary minus expression is an unsigned integral type after explicit conversions.
meta_impl: ume.getOperand().getExplicitlyConverted().getUnderlyingType().(IntegralType).isUnsigned()

meta_op: Exclude cases where the operand is a literal value.
meta_impl: not ume.getOperand() instanceof Literal

meta_op: Report the unary minus expression with a warning about applying negation to unsigned values.
meta_impl: select ume, "The unary minus operator should not be applied to an unsigned expression."

meta_op: Identify all comma expressions in the source code that are not part of macro invocations.
meta_impl:
from CommaExpr ce
where
  ce.fromSource() and
  not exists(MacroInvocation me | ce = me.getAnAffectedElement())

meta_op: Report each identified comma expression with a message indicating it violates AV Rule 168 by using the comma operator.
meta_impl: select ce, "AV Rule 168: The comma operator shall not be used."

meta_op: Define the predicate identifies relevant elements (classes, namespaces, enumerations, typedefs) and associates them with their blame element, kind, and name.
meta_impl:
predicate relevant(Element elem, Element blame, string kind, string name) {
  exists(Class c | elem = c and blame = c and kind = "class or struct" and name = c.getName()) // includes struct
  or
  exists(Namespace n |
    elem = n and blame = n.getADeclarationEntry() and kind = "namespace" and name = n.getName()
  )
  or
  exists(Enum e | elem = e and blame = e and kind = "enumeration" and name = e.getName())
  or
  exists(TypedefType t | elem = t and blame = t and kind = "typedef" and name = t.getName())
}

meta_op: Select elements with naming violations where the first word isn't capitalized or subsequent words aren't lowercase, excluding valid uppercase acronyms.
meta_impl:
from Element d, Element blame, Word w, int pos, string kind, string name
where
  relevant(d, blame, kind, name) and
  w = name.(Name).getWord(pos) and
  not (w.couldBeUppercaseAcronym() and w != name) and
  (
    pos = 0 and not w.isCapitalized()
    or
    pos > 0 and not w.isLowercase()
  )

meta_op: Report naming violations with a message indicating the proper capitalization rules for the element type according to AV Rule 50.
meta_impl:
select blame,
  "AV Rule 50: The first word of a " + kind +
    " will begin with an uppercase letter, and all other letters will be lowercase."

meta_op: Define a predicate determines if a declaration is relevant for AV Rule 52 checking, specifically for enumerator constants or const variables.
