Identify declarations that are relevant for AV Rule 52, extract their name words, and check if they violate the lowercase requirement while allowing uppercase acronyms in certain cases.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, Word w, string kind
where
  relevant(d, kind) and
  w = d.getName().(Name).getAWord() and
  not (w.couldBeUppercaseAcronym() and w != d.getName()) and
  not w.isLowercase()

meta_op: Select declarations that violate AV Rule 52 and generate a diagnostic message indicating the type of identifier (enumerator or constant) that should be lowercase.
meta_impl: select d, "AV Rule 52: identifiers for " + kind + " values shall be lowercase."

meta_op: Identify all declarations in source code along with their associated names.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, Name n
where
  d.fromSource() and
  n = d.getName()

meta_op: Extract individual words from the names of declarations.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, Name n, Word w
where
  d.fromSource() and
  n = d.getName() and
  w = n.getAWord()

meta_op: Filter for words that are definite acronyms but not in uppercase.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, Name n, Word w
where
  d.fromSource() and
  n = d.getName() and
  w = n.getAWord() and
  w.isDefiniteAcronym() and
  not w.isUppercase()

meta_op: Report declarations containing acronyms with incorrect case, providing a diagnostic message with the problematic acronym.
meta_impl:
select d,
  "AV Rule 49: acronyms in identifiers will be uppercase. Incorrect case for acronym " +
    w.toString() + "."

meta_op: Identify source-level declarations and extract their names for analysis.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, string name
where
  name = d.getName() and
  d.fromSource()

meta_op: For each character position in the declaration name, extract the current and next character.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, string name, string lowerCase, string upperCase, int pos
where
  name = d.getName() and
  d.fromSource() and
  lowerCase = name.charAt(pos) and
  upperCase = name.charAt(pos + 1)

meta_op: Check if the current character is lowercase and the next character is uppercase, indicating camel-case pattern.
meta_impl:
lowerCase.regexpMatch("[a-z]") and
  upperCase.regexpMatch("[A-Z]")

meta_op: Select declarations violating AV Rule 45 by containing camel-case patterns and generate diagnostic message.
meta_impl:
select d,
  "AV Rule 45: All words in an identifier will be separated by the underscore character. Camel-case is not allowed."

meta_op: Identify source code declarations that are defined in source files (not libraries or generated code).
meta_impl: d.fromSource()

meta_op: Check if the name of the declaration starts with an underscore character.
meta_impl: d.getName().matches("\\_%")

meta_op: Select declarations that violate the naming convention (starting with underscore) and generate a diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select d, "Identifiers will not begin with the underscore character."

meta_op: Identify files with header file extensions that are not the standard .h extension.
meta_impl:
from File f
where
  f.getExtension().toLowerCase() = ["h", "hpp"] and
  f.getExtension() != "h"

meta_op: Select files that violate AV Rule 53 by not using the standard .h extension for header files and generate a diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 53: Header files will always have a file name extension of .h."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact integration.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query for defects reported in external data that match the specific query path for JSF AV Rule 60.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.09 Style/AV Rule 60.ql"

meta_op: Select and report the defect along with its associated message from the external data.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Define canonicalization of declaration names by removing underscores and converting to lowercase to handle case differences and underscore variations.
Define canonicalization of declaration names by replacing similar-looking characters (0/O/D, 1/I/l, S/5, Z/2, n/h) to handle visual ambiguities.
meta_impl:
predicate canonicalName2(Declaration d, string canonical) {
  canonical =
    d.getName()
        .replaceAll("_", "")
        .replaceAll("0", "O")
        .replaceAll("D", "O")
        .replaceAll("1", "I")
        .replaceAll("l", "I")
        .replaceAll("S", "5")
        .replaceAll("Z", "2")
        .replaceAll("n", "h")
}

meta_op: Determine if two declarations have the same canonical name under either canonicalization scheme.
Check if a declaration is local to a specific function (either as a parameter or local variable).
Check if a declaration is a member variable of a specific struct.
meta_impl: predicate structLocal(Declaration d, Struct s) { d = s.getAMemberVariable() }

meta_op: Determine if two declarations are in compatible scopes for comparison (same struct, same function, or global scope).
meta_impl:
predicate compatibleScopes(Declaration d1, Declaration d2) {
  exists(Struct s | structLocal(d1, s) and structLocal(d2, s))
  or
  not structLocal(d1, _) and
  not structLocal(d2, _) and
  same(d1, d2) and
  (
    not local(d2, _)
    or
    exists(Function f | local(d1, f) and local(d2, f))
  )
}

meta_op: Select source-level declarations with similar names that violate AV Rule 48, considering their scope compatibility and type.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d1, Declaration d2
where
  d1.fromSource() and
  d2.fromSource() and
  same(d1, d2) and
  d1.getName() != d2.getName() and
  (
    d1 instanceof UserType and d2 instanceof UserType
    or
    d1 instanceof Variable and d2 instanceof Variable and compatibleScopes(d1, d2)
  )

meta_op: Report the violation with a message indicating the confusingly similar identifier.
meta_impl: select d1, "AV Rule 48: this identifier is too close to another identifier (" + d2.getName() + ")."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query external defect data that matches the specific query path for AV Rule 43.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.09 Style/AV Rule 43.ql"

meta_op: Select and report the defect data along with its associated message.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Define a class to represent parameters with valid line numbers, ensuring start and end lines are the same.
Check if a function's parameters conform to the style rules: first parameter on same line as function, others on separate lines.
Select functions with more than two parameters that violate the parameter declaration style rules.
meta_impl:
from Function f
where
  f.getNumberOfParameters() > 2 and
  f.hasDefinition() and
  not valid(f)

meta_op: Report functions violating AV Rule 58 with a message indicating non-conformance to parameter declaration style rules.
meta_impl:
select f,
  "AV Rule 58: functions with more than two parameters will conform to style rules for declaring parameters."

meta_op: Identify C++ files that do not have the .cpp extension.
meta_impl:
from CppFile f
where f.getExtension() != "cpp"

meta_op: Report files with incorrect extensions, providing a diagnostic message about the required .cpp extension.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 53: Implementation files will always have a file name extension of .cpp."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query external defect data that matches the specific query path for AV Rule 61.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.09 Style/AV Rule 61.ql"

meta_op: Select the defect data along with its associated diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Identify statements that are direct children of control flow structures (if, while, do-while, for) but are not enclosed in braces.
meta_impl:
from Stmt parent, Stmt child
where
  not child instanceof BlockStmt and
  (
    child = parent.(IfStmt).getThen()
    or
    child = parent.(WhileStmt).getStmt()
    or
    child = parent.(DoStmt).getStmt()
    or
    child = parent.(ForStmt).getStmt()
    or
    child = parent.(IfStmt).getElse() and not child instanceof IfStmt
  )

meta_op: Select the root cause of the identified statements and generate a diagnostic message recommending brace enclosure.
meta_impl:
select child.findRootCause(),
  "The statements forming the body of an if, else if, else, while, do...while or for statement shall always be enclosed in braces, even if the braces form an empty block."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query external defect data that matches the specific query path for AV Rule 44.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.09 Style/AV Rule 44.ql"

meta_op: Select the defect data along with its associated message for reporting.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query external defect data that matches the specific query path for AV Rule 41.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.09 Style/AV Rule 41.ql"

meta_op: Select the defect data and its associated message for reporting.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Identify include directives and extract the included header file name.
meta_impl:
from Include i, string name
where
  name = i.getIncludeText()

meta_op: Check if the header file name contains any of the forbidden character sequences: single quote, backslash, /*, //, or ".
Report the include directive with a warning about invalid character sequences in the header file name.
meta_impl: select i, "AV Rule 53.1: Invalid character sequence in header file name '" + name + "'."

meta_op: Identify all class members and their positions within a class declaration.
meta_impl:
from Class c, Declaration m1, Declaration m2, int pos1, int pos2
where
  m1 = c.getCanonicalMember(pos1) and
  m2 = c.getCanonicalMember(pos2)

meta_op: Ensure the first member appears before the second member in the class declaration.
meta_impl: pos1 < pos2

meta_op: Check for violations of the ordering rule where private members appear before protected or public members, or protected members appear before public members.
meta_impl:
(
    m1.hasSpecifier("private") and m2.hasSpecifier("protected")
    or
    m1.hasSpecifier("private") and m2.hasSpecifier("public")
    or
    m1.hasSpecifier("protected") and m2.hasSpecifier("public")
  )

meta_op: Select classes that violate the public-protected-private ordering rule and generate a diagnostic message.
meta_impl:
select c,
  "AV Rule 57: The public, protected, and private sections of a class will be declared in that order."

meta_op: Define the predicate to identify relevant declarations as either functions or variables and associate them with their kind.
Retrieve all words from the name of a declaration and filter out those that are uppercase acronyms or already lowercase.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, Word w, string kind
where
  relevant(d, kind) and
  w = d.getName().(Name).getAWord() and
  not (w.couldBeUppercaseAcronym() and w != d.getName()) and
  not w.isLowercase()

meta_op: Select declarations with non-lowercase names that violate the naming rule and generate a diagnostic message specifying the kind of declaration.
meta_impl: select d, "AV Rule 51: All letters contained in " + kind + " names will be lowercase."

meta_op: Check if an expression has a valid source location.
meta_impl: predicate hasLocation(Expr e) { e.getLocation().getStartLine() != 0 }

meta_op: Find the outermost conversion expression with a valid location for a given expression.
Calculate the column distance between the end of one expression and the beginning of another on the same line.
Calculate the column distance between the end of one expression and the end of another on the same line.
Calculate the column distance between the beginning of one expression and the beginning of another on the same line.
Check for incorrect spacing in pointer member access (->) operations.
meta_impl:
exists(Call c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getQualifier(), e) and
      e.getType() instanceof PointerType and
      not diffEndBegin(e, c, 3)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing in dot member access (.) operations.
meta_impl:
exists(Call c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getQualifier(), e) and
      not e.getType() instanceof PointerType and
      not diffEndBegin(e, c, 2)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing in pointer member access (->) operations on variable accesses.
meta_impl:
exists(VariableAccess c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getQualifier(), e) and
      e.getType() instanceof PointerType and
      not diffEndBegin(e, c, 3)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing in dot member access (.) operations on variable accesses.
meta_impl:
exists(VariableAccess c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getQualifier(), e) and
      not e.getType() instanceof PointerType and
      not diffEndBegin(e, c, 2)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing between unary operators and their operands for most unary operations.
meta_impl:
exists(UnaryOperation c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getOperand(), e) and
      (
        c instanceof ComplementExpr or
        c instanceof NotExpr or
        c instanceof UnaryMinusExpr or
        c instanceof UnaryPlusExpr or
        c instanceof AddressOfExpr
      ) and
      not diffBeginBegin(c, e, 1)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing between prefix increment operators and their operands.
meta_impl:
exists(PrefixIncrExpr c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getOperand(), e) and
      not diffBeginBegin(c, e, 2)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing between prefix decrement operators and their operands.
meta_impl:
exists(PrefixDecrExpr c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getOperand(), e) and
      not diffBeginBegin(c, e, 2)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing between pointer dereference operators and their operands.
meta_impl:
exists(PointerDereferenceExpr c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getChild(0), e) and
      not diffBeginBegin(c, e, 1)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing between postfix increment operators and their operands.
meta_impl:
exists(PostfixIncrExpr c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getOperand(), e) and
      not diffEndEnd(e, c, 2)
    )

meta_op: Check for incorrect spacing between postfix decrement operators and their operands.
meta_impl:
exists(PostfixDecrExpr c, Expr e |
      c = err and
      outermostConvWithLocation(c.getOperand(), e) and
      not diffEndEnd(e, c, 2)
    )

meta_op: Select all expressions violating AV Rule 63 and provide a diagnostic message.
meta_impl:
select err,
  "AV Rule 63: Spaces will not be used around '.' or '->', nor between unary operators and operands."

meta_op: Define when an element has a name longer than 64 characters and extract its significant prefix.
Extract the name of an element, whether it's a declaration or a namespace.
Identify pairs of elements that have different full names but share the same 64-character prefix.
Select elements that rely on more than 64 characters to distinguish them from another element, and report the violation.
meta_impl:
from Element e1, Element e2, string name
where
  clash(e1, e2) and
  elementName(e2, name)
select e1, "AV Rule 46: relies on more than 64 characters to separate from " + name

meta_op: Identify expressions that are directly contained within expression statements and capture their file locations.
Find pairs of expressions that appear in the same file and have overlapping line ranges, indicating they may be on the same or adjacent lines.
Select expressions that overlap with other expressions in their line ranges while excluding those from macro expansions.
meta_impl:
from Expr e
where overlappingExprs(e, _) and not e.isInMacroExpansion()

meta_op: Report expressions that violate AV Rule 42 by not being on separate lines, suggesting they should be moved to individual lines.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 42: Each expression-statement will be on a separate line."

meta_op: Define when a variable has external linkage, either through an 'extern' specifier or by being a non-const, non-static global variable.
meta_impl:
predicate externalLinkage(Variable v) {
  v.getADeclarationEntry().hasSpecifier("extern")
  or
  v instanceof GlobalVariable and
  not v.isConst() and
  not v.isStatic()
}

meta_op: Define when a global variable has internal linkage, either through being static or const without an 'extern' specifier.
Select variables that simultaneously have both internal and external linkage in the same translation unit, violating AV Rule 138.
meta_impl:
from Variable v
where externalLinkage(v) and internalLinkage(v)

meta_op: Report the violating variable with a diagnostic message about the AV Rule 138 violation regarding linkage conflicts.
meta_impl:
select v,
  "AV Rule 138: Identifiers shall not simultaneously have both internal and external linkage in the same translation unit."

meta_op: Identify declarations that appear in multiple files, excluding definition locations.
meta_impl:
predicate twoDeclarations(Declaration element, Location l1, Location l2) {
  l1 = element.getADeclarationLocation() and
  l2 = element.getADeclarationLocation() and
  l1.getFile() != l2.getFile() and
  l1 != element.getDefinitionLocation() and
  l2 != element.getDefinitionLocation()
}

meta_op: Find two distinct files containing declarations of the same object, using base names for differentiation.
meta_impl:
predicate twoDeclarationFilesWithDifferentNames(Declaration d, string f1, string f2) {
  f1 =
    min(string s, File f, Location l |
      twoDeclarations(d, l, _) and l.getFile() = f and s = f.getBaseName()
    |
      s
    ) and
  f2 =
    min(string s, File f, Location l |
      twoDeclarations(d, l, _) and l.getFile() = f and s = f.getBaseName() and s != f1
    |
      s
    )
}

meta_op: Find two distinct files containing declarations of the same object, using full file paths when base names are identical.
meta_impl:
predicate twoDeclarationFilesWithSameNames(Declaration d, string f1, string f2) {
  f1 =
    min(string s, File f, Location l |
      twoDeclarations(d, l, _) and l.getFile() = f and s = f.toString()
    |
      s
    ) and
  f2 =
    min(string s, File f, Location l |
      twoDeclarations(d, l, _) and l.getFile() = f and s = f.toString() and s != f1
    |
      s
    )
}

meta_op: Construct an error message showing the two files where the declaration appears, preferring base names when available.
Select declarations that violate AV Rule 139 by appearing in multiple files and generate the appropriate diagnostic message.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d, string msg
where
  twoDeclarations(d, _, _) and
  twoDeclarationFilesMessage(d, msg)
select d, "AV Rule 139: external objects will not be declared in more than one file. " + msg

meta_op: Identify all declarations that use the 'register' storage class specifier.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d
where d.hasSpecifier("register")

meta_op: Report each declaration that uses the 'register' specifier, suggesting compiler-based register allocation is preferable.
meta_impl:
select d,
  "The register storage class specifier should not be used; compilers can be trusted to decide whether to store a variable in a register."

meta_op: Define a class for local variables and parameters that can shadow other variables.
Define a predicate for when a local variable shadows a parameter within the same function.
Identify variable pairs where one variable hides another in an inner scope, excluding cases within macro expansions.
meta_impl:
from Variable v, Variable shadowed
where
  not v.getParentScope().(BlockStmt).isInMacroExpansion() and
  (
    v.(LocalVariableOrParameter).shadowsGlobal(shadowed) or
    localShadowsParameter(v, shadowed) or
    shadowing(v, shadowed)
  )

meta_op: Select and report variables that hide identifiers in outer scopes with a diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select v, "Identifiers in an inner scope should not hide identifiers in an outer scope."

meta_op: Check if a declaration is part of a declaration statement that contains multiple variable declarations on the same line.
meta_impl:
predicate isNonSolitary(Declaration d) {
  exists(DeclStmt ds, Variable v |
    ds.fromSource() and
    d = ds.getADeclaration() and
    d instanceof Variable and
    v = ds.getADeclaration() and
    v != d
  )

meta_op: Check if a global variable declaration shares the same line with another global variable declaration in the same file.
meta_impl:
or
  exists(GlobalVariable g |
    g.fromSource() and
    g.getLocation().getStartLine() = d.(GlobalVariable).getLocation().getStartLine() and
    g.getLocation().getFile() = d.getLocation().getFile() and
    g != d
  )

meta_op: Check if a field declaration shares the same line with another field declaration in the same file.
meta_impl:
or
  exists(Field f |
    f.fromSource() and
    f.getLocation().getStartLine() = d.(Field).getLocation().getStartLine() and
    f.getLocation().getFile() = d.getLocation().getFile() and
    f != d
  )

meta_op: Select all declarations that are non-solitary (i.e., share a line with other declarations) and report them as violations of AV Rule 152.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d
where isNonSolitary(d)
select d, "AV Rule 152: Multiple variable declarations shall not be allowed on the same line."

meta_op: Identify functions defined in source code that have more than 7 parameters.
meta_impl:
from Function f
where
  f.getNumberOfParameters() > 7 and
  f.fromSource()

meta_op: Report functions with excessive parameters, providing a warning message about AV Rule 110 compliance.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 110: Functions with more than 7 arguments will not be used."

meta_op: Define a return statement that returns an error code by checking for literals or symbolic constants, excluding zero in integral contexts.
Identify functions that have both error-returning and non-error-returning statements, indicating they may return error information.
Find calls to error-returning functions that are used in void contexts where the return value is ignored.
meta_impl:
from FunctionReturningErrorCode frec, Call c
where
  c = frec.getACallToThisFunction() and
  c instanceof ExprInVoidContext

meta_op: Report calls that ignore error return values with a diagnostic message referencing AV Rule 115.
meta_impl: select c, "AV Rule 115: If a function returns error information, it will be tested."

meta_op: Identify declaration statements that contain function declarations.
meta_impl:
from DeclStmt ds
where
  ds.getADeclaration() instanceof Function

meta_op: Exclude function declarations that are part of macro expansions.
meta_impl: not ds.isInMacroExpansion()

meta_op: Exclude function declarations that originate from macro invocations.
meta_impl: not exists(MacroInvocation mi | mi.getLocation() = ds.getADeclarationEntry().getLocation())

meta_op: Select and report declaration statements that contain function declarations in block scope, recommending file scope instead.
meta_impl: select ds, "Functions should be declared at file scope, not inside blocks."

meta_op: Identify functions that are defined in source code and have variable number of arguments.
meta_impl:
from Function f
where f.fromSource() and f.hasSpecifier("varargs")

meta_op: Report each identified function with a warning message about the violation of AV Rule 108 regarding variable arguments.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 108: Functions with variable number of arguments shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify functions that have multiple exit points by finding different predecessor statements within the same function.
meta_impl:
from Function f, Stmt s1, Stmt s2
where
  s1 = f.getAPredecessor().getEnclosingStmt() and
  s2 = f.getAPredecessor().getEnclosingStmt() and
  s1 != s2

meta_op: Report functions that violate the single exit point rule with a diagnostic message indicating the AV Rule 113 violation.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 113: Functions will have a single exit point."

meta_op: Define a custom PointsToExpr class that identifies expressions returned from functions and have pointer values.
Identify return statements where the returned expression points to a stack-allocated variable in the same function.
meta_impl:
from ReturnPointsToExpr ret, StackVariable dest
where
  ret.pointsTo() = dest and
  ret.getReturnStmt().getParentStmt().getEnclosingFunction() = dest.getFunction()

meta_op: Report the violating return statement with a diagnostic message about returning pointers to local objects.
meta_impl:
select ret.getReturnStmt(),
  "AV Rule 111: A function shall not return a pointer or reference to a non-static local object."

meta_op: Identify source functions that are defined in the codebase (not from libraries or system headers).
meta_impl:
from Function f
where
  f.fromSource()

meta_op: Detect functions that call themselves either directly or indirectly through a chain of calls.
meta_impl: f.calls+(f)

meta_op: Select and report functions that exhibit recursive behavior with a diagnostic message about avoiding recursion.
meta_impl: select f, "Functions shall not call themselves, either directly or indirectly."

meta_op: Identify functions that are missing return statements by checking for non-void return types and control flow paths that end without returning a value.
meta_impl:
predicate functionsMissingReturnStmt(Function f, ControlFlowNode blame) {
  f.fromSource() and
  exists(Type returnType |
    returnType = f.getUnspecifiedType() and
    not returnType instanceof VoidType and
    not returnType instanceof TypeTemplateParameter
  ) and
  exists(ReturnStmt s |
    f.getAPredecessor() = s and
    (
      blame = s.getAPredecessor() and
      count(blame.getASuccessor()) = 1
      or
      blame = s and
      exists(ControlFlowNode pred | pred = s.getAPredecessor() | count(pred.getASuccessor()) != 1)
    )
  )
}

meta_op: Exclude functions that were imperfectly extracted by the compiler, including those with errors, multiple types, or containing assembly statements.
meta_impl:
predicate functionImperfectlyExtracted(Function f) {
  exists(CompilerError e | f.getBlock().getLocation().subsumes(e.getLocation()))
  or
  f.hasErrors()
  or
  count(f.getType()) > 1
  or
  exists(AsmStmt asm | asm.getEnclosingFunction() = f)
}

meta_op: Select statements in functions with missing returns that are reachable and not from uninstantiated templates, while ensuring the function was perfectly extracted.
meta_impl:
from Stmt stmt, string msg, Function f, ControlFlowNode blame
where
  functionsMissingReturnStmt(f, blame) and
  reachable(blame) and
  not functionImperfectlyExtracted(f) and
  not f.isFromUninstantiatedTemplate(_) and
  (blame = stmt or blame.(Expr).getEnclosingStmt() = stmt)

meta_op: Construct a diagnostic message indicating the function's name and expected return type where the missing return occurs.
meta_impl:
msg =
    "Function " + f.getName() + " should return a value of type " + f.getType().getName() +
      " but does not return a value here"

meta_op: Report the problematic statement along with the constructed diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select stmt, msg

meta_op: Identify all bit field declarations in the code.
meta_impl: from BitField bf
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 155.ql

meta_op: Filter out bit fields that are based on enum types or have zero declared bits.
meta_impl:
where
  not bf.getUnderlyingType() instanceof Enum and
  not bf.getDeclaredNumBits() = 0
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 155.ql

meta_op: Report bit fields that violate AV Rule 155 by being used purely for space-saving purposes.
meta_impl: select bf, "AV Rule 155: Bit fields will not be used purely to save space."
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 155.ql

meta_op: Identify all unions that are defined in source code.
meta_impl:
from Union u
where u.fromSource()
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 153.ql

meta_op: Report each identified union with a message indicating that unions should not be used according to AV Rule 153.
meta_impl: select u, "AV Rule 153: Unions shall not be used."
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 153.ql

meta_op: Identify member declarations in structures or classes that have generated names indicating they are unnamed.
meta_impl:
from Declaration m
where
  m.isMember() and
  m.getName().matches("(\%")
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 156.ql

meta_op: Exclude bitfield members that have a declared bit width of 0 from the unnamed member check.
meta_impl: not m.(BitField).getDeclaredNumBits() = 0
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 156.ql

meta_op: Select and report unnamed members that violate the naming requirement, providing a diagnostic message about AV Rule 156.
meta_impl:
select m,
  "AV Rule 156: All the members of a structure (or class) shall be named and shall only be accessed via their names."
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 156.ql

meta_op: Identify bit fields that are not explicitly unsigned or of enumeration type, excluding certain special cases.
meta_impl:
from BitField bf
where
  not bf.getUnspecifiedType().(IntegralType).isUnsigned() and
  not bf.getUnderlyingType() instanceof Enum and
  not bf.getUnspecifiedType() instanceof BoolType and
  not bf.getType().hasName("BOOL") and
  not bf.getDeclaredNumBits() = bf.getType().getSize() * 8 and
  not bf.isAnonymous()
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 154.ql

meta_op: Report the problematic bit fields with a warning message suggesting the use of unsigned integral or enumeration types.
meta_impl:
select bf,
  "Bit field " + bf.getName() + " of type " + bf.getUnderlyingType().getName() +
    " should have explicitly unsigned integral or enumeration type."
reference_path: jsf/4.20 Unions and Bit Fields/AV Rule 154.ql

meta_op: Define a compiler warning class for late template specializations that occur after their use or make uses ambiguous.
Select all instances of late template specialization warnings and format an AV Rule 104 violation message.
meta_impl:
from WarningLateTemplateSpecialization warning
select warning,
  "AV Rule 104: A template specialization shall be declared before its use; " + warning.getMessage()
    + "."

meta_op: Check if an enum constant has an explicit initializer in the source code.
meta_impl: predicate hasInitializer(EnumConstant c) { c.getInitializer().fromSource() }

meta_op: Check if an enum constant has an initializer that is not just a reference to another constant in the same enum.
Check if an enum constant has an initializer that is a reference to another constant in the same enum.
Get the nth enum constant in an enum that does not have a reference initializer, ordered by position and location.
Check if an enum constant is not the last one in its enum that has a non-reference initializer.
Check if an enum has at least one constant with an implicit value that is not the last constant (allowing the last to be used for counting).
Select enums that have irregular initialization patterns where explicit initializers are used on non-first/non-last constants while other constants have implicit values.
meta_impl:
from Enum e, int i
where
  hasNonReferenceInitializer(e.getEnumConstant(i)) and
  i != 0 and
  exists(e.getEnumConstant(i + 1)) and
  enumThatHasConstantWithImplicitValue(e)

meta_op: Report enums with irregular initialization patterns, suggesting they should only use explicit values on the first constant or all constants.
meta_impl:
select e,
  "In an enumerator list, the = construct should not be used to explicitly initialize members other than the first, unless all items are explicitly initialized."

meta_op: Determine if a variable access defines a specific variable (conservative approach).
meta_impl: predicate defines(VariableAccess va, Variable lv) { va.getTarget() = lv and va.isLValue() }

meta_op: Determine if a control flow node uses a specific variable, excluding sizeof operations (conservative approach).
Check if there is a path from a variable's declaration to a node where the variable is definitely not used or defined before reaching that node.
Check if two statements are neighboring statements within the same block.
Select local variables from source code where the declaration and definition are not neighboring statements and there's no use of the variable along the path from declaration to definition.
meta_impl:
from LocalVariable lv, VariableAccess def, DeclStmt d
where
  lv.fromSource() and
  d.getADeclaration() = lv and
  noDefUsePath(lv, def) and
  defines(def, lv) and
  not neighbouringStmts(d, def.getEnclosingStmt()) and
  not exists(ControlFlowNode use | noDefUsePath(lv, use) and uses(use, lv))

meta_op: Report the variable definition that violates AV Rule 143 by being introduced before meaningful initialization.
meta_impl:
select def,
  "AV Rule 143: Variables will not be introduced until they can be initialized with meaningful values."

meta_op: Determine if a control flow node defines a given variable by checking for an LValue variable access.
Determine if a control flow node uses a given variable by checking for an RValue variable access, excluding sizeof operators.
Identify paths from a local variable declaration to a node where the variable is definitely not defined before use, considering declarations without initializers.
Check if a type is an aggregate type (class or array).
meta_impl: predicate isAggregateType(Type t) { t instanceof Class or t instanceof ArrayType }

meta_op: Identify uses of local variables that have not been previously defined, excluding aggregate types and va_list variables.
Identify potentially uninitialized global variables by checking for RValue accesses of globals without initializers and non-extern specifiers.
Select elements that represent either undefined local variable uses or uninitialized global variables.
meta_impl:
from Element elt
where undefinedLocalUse(elt) or uninitialisedGlobal(elt)

meta_op: Report elements that violate AV Rule 142 with a diagnostic message about uninitialized variable use.
meta_impl: select elt, "AV Rule 142: All variables shall be initialized before use."

meta_op: Define a predicate to select a representative file for a namespace by choosing the file with the lexicographically smallest absolute path that contains any declaration from the namespace.
Identify namespaces that are nested more than two levels deep by checking for the existence of a great-grandparent namespace.
meta_impl:
from Namespace ns, File rep
where
  exists(ns.getParentNamespace().getParentNamespace().getParentNamespace()) and
  namespaceRepresentative(ns, rep)

meta_op: Report the violation by selecting the representative file and generating a message indicating the namespace is nested too deeply.
meta_impl: select rep, "AV Rule 99: namespace " + ns.toString() + " is nested more than two levels deep."

meta_op: Define a predicate to identify forbidden declarations in header files, which includes non-const variables and non-inline functions.
Select declarations located in header files that match the forbidden criteria and report them with a warning message.
meta_impl:
from Declaration d
where
  d.getDefinitionLocation().getFile() instanceof HeaderFile and
  forbidden(d)
select d.getDefinitionLocation(),
  "AV Rule 38: header files will not contain non-const variable definitions or function definitions."

meta_op: Define a function to identify potential header guard macro strings in a header file by matching macro definitions with their bodies.
Create an option type to represent optional preprocessor directives with associated utility methods for string representation and location retrieval.
Construct detailed diagnostic messages for incorrect header guard cases by analyzing the relationship between #ifndef and matching #endif directives.
meta_impl:
string extraDetail(
  HeaderFile hf, SomePreprocessorDirective detail1, SomePreprocessorDirective detail2
) {
  exists(string s, PreprocessorEndif endif, PreprocessorDirective ifndef |
    startsWithIfndef(hf, ifndef, s) and endif.getIf() = ifndef
  |
    detail1.getPreprocessorDirective() = endif and
    detail2.getPreprocessorDirective() = ifndef and
    if not endsWithEndif(hf, endif)
    then result = " ($@ matching $@ occurs before the end of the file)."
    else
      if exists(Macro m | m.getFile() = hf and m.getHead() = s)
      then result = " (#define " + s + " needs to appear immediately after #ifndef " + s + ")."
      else
        if strictcount(possibleGuard(hf, _)) = 1
        then
          result =
            " (" + possibleGuard(hf, _) + " should appear in the #ifndef rather than " + s + ")."
        else
          if strictcount(possibleGuard(hf, "")) = 1
          then
            result =
              " (" + possibleGuard(hf, "") + " should appear in the #ifndef rather than " + s + ")."
          else result = " (the macro " + s + " is checked for, but is not defined)."
  )
}

meta_op: Identify header files that use a specific macro without being include-guarded, through macro accesses within the file.
Detect files that define a specific macro through explicit macro definitions.
Detect files that undefine a specific macro through explicit #undef directives.
Identify files that both define and undefine a specific macro, typically indicating temporary macro usage.
Detect header files that exhibit x-macro patterns where macros are temporarily defined by includers or the file itself.
Select non-include-guarded header files that need header guards, excluding x-macros, single-inclusion files, and import-only cases.
meta_impl:
from
  HeaderFile hf, string detail, MaybePreprocessorDirective detail1,
  MaybePreprocessorDirective detail2
where
  not hf instanceof IncludeGuardedHeader and
  (
    if exists(extraDetail(hf, _, _))
    then detail = extraDetail(hf, detail1, detail2)
    else (
      detail = "." and
      detail1 instanceof NoPreprocessorDirective and
      detail2 instanceof NoPreprocessorDirective
    )
  ) and
  not hf.noTopLevelCode() and
  not hasXMacro(hf) and
  not forex(Include i | i.getIncludedFile() = hf | i instanceof Import) and
  not strictcount(Include i | i.getIncludedFile() = hf) = 1

meta_op: Report missing header guard violations with contextual details about incorrect guard patterns when present.
meta_impl:
select hf, "This header file should contain a header guard to prevent multiple inclusion" + detail,
  detail1, detail1.toString(), detail2, detail2.toString()

meta_op: Identify all include directives in the codebase.
meta_impl: from Include i

meta_op: Filter include directives that use the angle bracket notation for header files.
meta_impl: where i.getIncludeText().matches("<%"")

meta_op: Report the include directive with a message indicating compliance with AV Rule 33 regarding header file inclusion notation.
meta_impl: select i, "AV Rule 33: the #include <filename.h> notation shall be used."

meta_op: Identify preprocessor directives that are either #ifndef or #endif.
meta_impl:
from PreprocessorDirective directive
where
  (directive instanceof PreprocessorIfndef or directive instanceof PreprocessorEndif)

meta_op: Exclude directives that are part of a correct include guard as defined in AV Rule 27.
meta_impl: not exists(CorrectIncludeGuard cig | directive = cig.getIfndef() or directive = cig.getEndif())

meta_op: Select and report the violating directives with a message about AV Rule 28 compliance.
meta_impl:
select directive,
  "AV Rule 28: the #ifndef and #endif directives will only be used as defined in AV Rule 27."

meta_op: Identify all macro definitions in the codebase that are not part of a correct include guard pattern.
meta_impl:
from Macro macro
where not exists(CorrectIncludeGuard cig | macro = cig.getDefine())

meta_op: Report macros that violate AV Rule 31 by not being part of include guard protection, providing a warning message about proper usage of #define directives.
meta_impl:
select macro,
  "AV Rule 31: The #define directive will only be used as part of the technique to prevent multiple inclusions of the same header file."

meta_op: Identify all include directives and their corresponding included files.
meta_impl:
from Include i, File f
where
  f = i.getIncludedFile()

meta_op: Extract and normalize the file extension of the included file.
meta_impl: extension = f.getExtension().toLowerCase()

meta_op: Filter out files with allowed non-header extensions.
meta_impl:
extension != "def" and
  extension != "inc" and
  extension != "inl" and
  extension != "tcc" and
  extension != "tpp" and
  extension != "txx" and
  extension != "xpm"

meta_op: Exclude autogenerated files from the analysis.
meta_impl: not f instanceof AutogeneratedFile

meta_op: Exclude files that are already classified as header files.
meta_impl: not f instanceof HeaderFile

meta_op: Select include directives that violate the header-only rule and generate an appropriate diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select i, "The #include pre-processor directive should only be used to include header files."

meta_op: Identify macro definitions that represent inline macros by checking for function-like syntax in the macro head.
meta_impl:
from Macro m
where m.getHead().matches("%(%")

meta_op: Report identified inline macros with a diagnostic message indicating violation of the rule against using #define for inline macros.
meta_impl: select m, "The #define pre-processor directive shall not be used to create inline macros."

meta_op: Define a macro that represents a simple constant definition, excluding function-like macros and those with special characters in their bodies.
Identify common macros that should be excluded from the analysis, such as NULL.
Select constant-defining macros that are not common macros and come from source code.
meta_impl:
from ConstantDefMacro m
where
  not commonMacro(m.getHead()) and
  m.fromSource()

meta_op: Report the violation with a message indicating that #define should not be used for constant values.
meta_impl: select m, "The #define pre-processor directive shall not be used to define constant values."

meta_op: Identify all preprocessor directives that are not allowed according to AV Rule 26.
meta_impl:
from PreprocessorDirective directive
where
  not directive instanceof PreprocessorIfndef and
  not directive instanceof PreprocessorEndif and
  not directive instanceof Macro and
  not directive instanceof Include

meta_op: Report the violating preprocessor directives with a message indicating the rule violation.
meta_impl:
select directive,
  "AV Rule 26: only the #ifndef, #endif, #define and #include directives shall be used."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and header file multiple inclusion checks.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import semmle.code.cpp.headers.MultipleInclusion

meta_op: Identify header files with bad include guards that don't use the standard #ifndef/#define/#endif pattern.
meta_impl: from BadIncludeGuard bad

meta_op: Select and report header files violating AV Rule 27 by using non-standard multiple inclusion prevention techniques.
meta_impl:
select bad.blame(),
  "AV Rule 27: techniques other than #ifndef/#define/#endif will not be used to prevent multiple inclusions of header files."

meta_op: Identify functions from the standard library that are considered dangerous and should not be used.
meta_impl:
from Function f
where
  f.getName() = ["abort", "exit", "getenv", "system"] and
  f.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%stdlib.h")

meta_op: Select all calls to the identified dangerous functions and generate a warning message about their usage.
meta_impl:
select f.getACallToThisFunction(),
  "The library functions abort, exit, getenv and system from library <stdlib.h> should not be used."

meta_op: Identify macro definitions of 'errno' and their invocations.
meta_impl: errno.(Macro).getHead() = "errno" and use = errno.(Macro).getAnInvocation()

meta_op: Identify variable declarations of 'errno' and their accesses.
meta_impl: errno.(Variable).hasName("errno") and use = errno.(Variable).getAnAccess()

meta_op: Ensure the 'errno' definition comes from an errno.h file.
meta_impl: errno.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%errno.h")

meta_op: Select all uses of 'errno' (either macro invocations or variable accesses) that originate from an errno.h file.
meta_impl:
from Locatable errno, Locatable use
where
  (
    errno.(Macro).getHead() = "errno" and use = errno.(Macro).getAnInvocation()
    or
    errno.(Variable).hasName("errno") and use = errno.(Variable).getAnAccess()
  ) and
  errno.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%errno.h")

meta_op: Report each violation with a message indicating the use of errno violates AV Rule 17.
meta_impl: select use, "AV Rule 17: The error indicator errno shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify all include directives that reference the locale.h header file.
meta_impl:
from Include incl
where incl.getIncludedFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%locale.h")

meta_op: Report the violating include directive with a message about AV Rule 19 compliance.
meta_impl: select incl, "AV Rule 19: <locale.h> and the setlocale function shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify all include directives in the codebase.
meta_impl: from Include incl

meta_op: Filter includes that reference the stdio.h library by matching the absolute path of the included file.
meta_impl: where incl.getIncludedFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%stdio.h")

meta_op: Report each violation with a message indicating the use of the prohibited stdio.h library according to AV Rule 22.
meta_impl: select incl, "AV Rule 22: The input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify macro invocations of 'offsetof' with specific pattern matching in the macro head.
meta_impl:
from Macro offsetof
where
  offsetof.getHead().matches("offsetof(%,%)")

meta_op: Filter macro invocations to only those originating from the 'stddef.h' library file.
meta_impl: offsetof.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%stddef.h")

meta_op: Select and report all invocations of the 'offsetof' macro from 'stddef.h' with a violation message.
meta_impl:
select offsetof.getAnInvocation(),
  "AV Rule 18: The macro offsetof, in library <stddef.h>, shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify all include directives in the codebase.
meta_impl: from Include incl

meta_op: Filter include directives that target the time.h library by checking the absolute path of included files.
meta_impl: where incl.getIncludedFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%time.h")

meta_op: Report each violation with a diagnostic message about the forbidden time.h usage.
meta_impl: select incl, "AV Rule 25: The time handling functions of library <time.h> shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify all include directives in the codebase.
meta_impl: from Include incl

meta_op: Filter includes that reference the signal.h header file using a path pattern match.
meta_impl: where incl.getIncludedFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%signal.h")

meta_op: Report each violation with a message indicating the AV Rule 21 violation for signal.h usage.
meta_impl: select incl, "AV Rule 21: The signal handling facilities of <signal.h> shall not be used."

meta_op: Define a class to identify the setjmp macro by matching its invocation pattern and header file.
Define a class to identify the longjmp function by its name, parameter count, and header file.
Locate all uses of setjmp macro invocations or longjmp function calls that violate the coding rule.
meta_impl:
from Setjmp setjmp, Longjmp longjmp, Locatable use
where
  use = setjmp.getAnInvocation() or
  use = longjmp.getACallToThisFunction()

meta_op: Report each violation with a standard warning message citing AV Rule 20.
meta_impl: select use, "AV Rule 20: The setjmp macro and the longjmp function shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify functions with specific names (atof, atoi, atol) from the stdlib.h library.
meta_impl:
from Function f
where
  f.getName() = ["atof", "atoi", "atol"] and
  f.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%stdlib.h")

meta_op: Select all calls to the identified functions and generate an error message about their usage.
meta_impl:
select f.getACallToThisFunction(),
  "AV Rule 23: The library functions atof, atoi and atol from library <stdlib.h> shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify all elements in the code that are either try statements or throw expressions.
meta_impl:
from Element e
where e instanceof TryStmt or e instanceof ThrowExpr

meta_op: Report the identified elements with a message indicating violation of AV Rule 208 regarding C++ exceptions.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 208: C++ exceptions shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify all goto statements in the source code that originate from source files.
meta_impl:
from GotoStmt s
where
  s.fromSource()

meta_op: Filter out goto statements that are used to break from nested loops, as they may be justified.
meta_impl: not s.breaksFromNestedLoops()

meta_op: Select and report remaining goto statements with a warning about their usage according to AV Rule 189.
meta_impl: select s, "AV Rule 189: The goto statement shall not be used."

meta_op: Identify switch statements over enumerations that lack a default case.
meta_impl:
from EnumSwitch es
where
  not es.hasDefaultCase()

meta_op: Find enumeration constants that are missing from the switch cases.
meta_impl:
from EnumSwitch es, EnumConstant ec
where
  ec = es.getAMissingCase()

meta_op: Select the first missing enumeration constant (by declaration order) from switches without default cases.
meta_impl:
from EnumSwitch es, EnumConstant ec
where
  not es.hasDefaultCase() and
  ec = es.getAMissingCase() and
  not exists(EnumConstant ec2, int i, int j |
    ec2 = es.getAMissingCase() and
    ec2 = ec2.getDeclaringEnum().getEnumConstant(i) and
    ec = ec.getDeclaringEnum().getEnumConstant(j) and
    i < j
  )

meta_op: Report switches missing default cases with a diagnostic message indicating the first missing enumeration constant.
meta_impl:
select es,
  "AV Rule 195: all switch statements that do not intend to test for every enumeration value shall contain a final default clause. This statement is missing a case for "
    + ec.getName()

meta_op: Identify loop counters in the program that have floating-point underlying types.
meta_impl:
from LoopCounter lc
where lc.getUnderlyingType() instanceof FloatingPointType

meta_op: Report loop counters with floating-point types, suggesting they should not be used as loop counters due to potential precision issues.
meta_impl: select lc, "Floating point variables should not be used as loop counters."

meta_op: Define what constitutes a valid loop update expression in a for loop, including increment/decrement operations, assignment expressions, and function calls (to account for overloaded operators).
Select for loops from source code that have an update expression which does not meet the criteria of a valid loop update.
meta_impl:
from ForStmt for
where
  for.fromSource() and
  not isValidLoopUpdate(for.getUpdate())

meta_op: Report the for loop that violates AV Rule 199, indicating that its increment expression does not properly update a single loop parameter.
meta_impl:
select for,
  "AV Rule 199: The increment expression in a for loop will only update a single for loop parameter."

meta_op: Define a predicate to check if an IfStmt is commented, either directly or with a comment on the same line after the if statement.
Select IfStmt instances that are part of an if-else if chain, lack an else clause, and are not commented, to flag violations of AV Rule 192.
meta_impl:
from IfStmt i
where
  i.fromSource() and
  exists(IfStmt i2 | i2.getElse() = i) and
  not i.hasElse() and
  not isCommented(i)

meta_op: Report the violating IfStmt with a message indicating the need for an else clause or a comment explaining its absence.
meta_impl:
select i,
  "AV Rule 192: All if-else if chains will have an else clause or a comment explaining its absence."

meta_op: Identify for loops in source code that have either null initialization or null increment expressions.
meta_impl:
from ForStmt f
where
  f.fromSource() and
  (not exists(f.getInitialization()) or not exists(f.getUpdate()))

meta_op: Report the identified for loops with a warning message indicating they violate AV Rule 200 by having null initialize or increment expressions.
meta_impl: select f, "AV Rule 200: Every for loop will have non-null initialize and increment expressions."

meta_op: Identify when a variable is modified within the body of a for loop where it serves as the iteration variable.
meta_impl:
pragma[noopt]
predicate loopModification(ForStmt for, Variable loopVariable, VariableAccess acc) {
  loopVariable = for.getAnIterationVariable() and
  acc = loopVariable.getAnAccess() and
  acc.isModified() and
  exists(Stmt stmt | acc.getEnclosingStmt() = stmt and stmtInForBody(stmt, for))
}

meta_op: Determine if a statement is contained within the body of a for loop, either directly or through nested statements.
Ensure field accesses within loop conditions and modifications refer to the same object instance for fields.
meta_impl:
(
    loopVariable instanceof Field
    implies
    exists(Variable obj |
      simpleFieldAccess(obj, loopVariable, acc) and
      simpleFieldAccess(obj, loopVariable, for.getCondition().getAChild*())
    )
  )

meta_op: Exclude cases already detected by nested loop variable modification violations from NestedLoopSameVar query.
meta_impl:
not exists(ForStmt inner |
    nestedForViolation(inner, loopVariable, for) and
    (
      acc.getParent*() = inner or
      acc.getParent*() = inner.getInitialization()
    )
  )

meta_op: Select and report variable accesses that modify for loop iteration variables within the loop body, with appropriate diagnostic message.
meta_impl: select acc, "Loop counters should not be modified in the body of the $@.", for.getStmt(), "loop"

meta_op: Identify switch statements that originate from source code (not generated by macros).
meta_impl:
from SwitchStmt s
where
  s.fromSource() and
  not exists(s.getGeneratingMacro())

meta_op: Count the number of non-default cases in each switch statement.
meta_impl: count(SwitchCase sc | sc.getSwitchStmt() = s and not sc instanceof DefaultCase) < 2

meta_op: Select switch statements with fewer than two non-default cases and suggest refactoring to if statements.
meta_impl:
select s,
  "This switch statement should either handle more cases, or be rewritten as an if statement."

meta_op: Define API entry points which are considered reachable by default, including main function and public member functions.
Identify unused functions that are not API entry points, have no callers, and have definitions.
Detect unreachable statements by checking if they are not successors in the control flow graph of their containing block.
meta_impl: predicate unreachableStmt(Stmt s) { not s.getControlFlowScope().getBlock().getASuccessor*() = s }

meta_op: Select control flow nodes that are either unreachable statements or unused functions.
meta_impl:
from ControlFlowNode n
where unreachableStmt(n) or unusedFunction(n)

meta_op: Report the unreachable code with a message referencing AV Rule 186.
meta_impl: select n, "AV Rule 186: There shall be no unreachable code."

meta_op: Identify all label statements in the code that are not targets of goto statements used for breaking from nested loops.
meta_impl:
from LabelStmt l
where not exists(GotoStmt g | g.breaksFromNestedLoops() and g.getTarget() = l)

meta_op: Report the identified label statements with a warning message about AV Rule 188 violation (labels should only be used in switch statements).
meta_impl: select l, "AV Rule 188: Labels will not be used, except in switch statements."

meta_op: Identify switch statements in the source code that are explicitly written in the source files.
meta_impl:
from SwitchStmt s
where
  s.fromSource()

meta_op: Check if the expression in a switch statement has an underlying Boolean type.
meta_impl: s.getExpr().getUnderlyingType() instanceof BoolType

meta_op: Select and report switch statements that violate AV Rule 195 by using a Boolean expression, along with a descriptive warning message.
meta_impl: select s, "AV Rule 195: A switch expression will not represent a Boolean value."

meta_op: Define what constitutes a valid initialization statement in a for loop, which can be either a declaration statement or an assignment expression.
Identify for loops in source code that have initialization statements which do not meet the validity criteria.
meta_impl:
from ForStmt for
where
  for.fromSource() and
  not isValidLoopInitialization(for.getInitialization())

meta_op: Report for loops with invalid initialization statements, providing a warning message about the violation of AV Rule 198.
meta_impl:
select for,
  "AV Rule 198: The initialization statement in a for loop will only initialize a single loop parameter."

meta_op: Filter source code statements that originate from source files and exclude empty statements.
meta_impl:
s.fromSource() and
  not s instanceof EmptyStmt

meta_op: Exclude control flow statements (labels, jumps, returns, and control structures) from the analysis targets.
meta_impl:
not s instanceof LabelStmt and
  not s instanceof JumpStmt and
  not s instanceof ReturnStmt and
  not s instanceof ControlStructure

meta_op: Exclude block statements from analysis since their child statements will be analyzed individually.
meta_impl: not s instanceof BlockStmt

meta_op: Check if the statement is pure (has no side effects) to identify violations of AV Rule 187.
meta_impl: s.isPure()

meta_op: Select statements violating AV Rule 187 and generate an error message.
meta_impl: select s, "AV Rule 187: All non-null statements shall potentially have a side-effect."

meta_op: Identify all continue statements in the source code that originate from source files.
meta_impl:
from ContinueStmt s
where s.fromSource()

meta_op: Report each identified continue statement with a message indicating violation of AV Rule 190.
meta_impl: select s, "AV Rule 190: The continue statement shall not be used."

meta_op: Define a predicate to determine if a statement is the terminating statement of another statement, including through nested blocks.
Select all break statements in source code that are not part of switch case termination.
meta_impl:
from BreakStmt s
where
  s.fromSource() and
  not exists(SwitchCase sc | isTerminatingStmt(sc.getLastStmt(), s))

meta_op: Report each violating break statement with a message indicating non-compliance with AV Rule 191.
meta_impl: select s, "AV Rule 191: The break statement shall not be used."

meta_op: Define when a switch case is the last default case in a switch statement by checking if it's a default case and no other cases follow it.
Select switch cases that are from source code, have statements, are not the last default case, and do not terminate with a break or return statement.
meta_impl:
from SwitchCase sc
where
  sc.fromSource() and
  exists(sc.getAStmt()) and
  not lastDefaultCase(sc) and
  not sc.terminatesInBreakStmt() and
  not sc.terminatesInReturnStmt()

meta_op: Report switch cases that violate AV Rule 193 by not being terminated with a break statement, providing a warning message.
meta_impl:
select sc,
  "AV Rule 193: Every non-empty case clause in a switch statement shall be terminated with a break statement."

meta_op: Identify literals that are of type wchar_t or arrays with base type wchar_t.
meta_impl:
from Literal l
where
  l.getType() instanceof Wchar_t or
  l.getType().(ArrayType).getBaseType().getUnspecifiedType() instanceof Wchar_t

meta_op: Report the identified literals with a message indicating violation of AV Rule 13 regarding multi-byte characters and wide string literals.
meta_impl: select l, "AV Rule 13: Multi-byte characters and wide string literals will not be used."

meta_op: Identify source code literals that have lowercase suffix letters.
meta_impl:
from Literal l
where
  l.fromSource() and
  l.getValueText().regexpMatch(".*[ul][uUlL]*\\s*")

meta_op: Report literals with lowercase suffixes, providing a diagnostic message about the coding standard violation.
meta_impl: select l, "AV Rule 14: Literal suffixes shall use uppercase rather than lowercase letters."

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query for defects related to external data that match the specific JSF AV Rule 12 query path.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.04 Environment/AV Rule 12.ql"

meta_op: Select the identified defects and their associated messages for reporting.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Import necessary CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query for defects in external data that match the specific AV Rule 9 query path.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.04 Environment/AV Rule 9.ql"

meta_op: Select and report the defects found, including their associated messages.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Import required CodeQL libraries for C++ analysis and external artifact handling.
meta_impl:
import cpp
import external.ExternalArtifact

meta_op: Query defect data from external sources where the query path matches the specified JSF AV Rule 11.
meta_impl:
from DefectExternalData d
where d.getQueryPath() = "jsf/4.04 Environment/AV Rule 11.ql"

meta_op: Select and report the external defect data along with its associated message.
meta_impl: select d, d.getMessage()

meta_op: Define a predicate to check if one type is a subtype of another by examining their class inheritance hierarchy.
Identify expressions in source code that involve downcasting from a base type to a derived type, either through references or pointers.
meta_impl:
from Expr e, Type declared, Type converted
where
  e.fromSource() and
  declared = e.getUnderlyingType() and
  converted = e.getActualType() and
  (
    subtype(converted.(ReferenceType).getBaseType(), declared.(ReferenceType).getBaseType())
    or
    subtype(converted.(PointerType).getBaseType(), declared.(PointerType).getBaseType())
  )

meta_op: Report violations of AV Rule 178 by selecting expressions that perform downcasting outside of virtual functions.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 178: Down casting shall only be allowed through virtual functions."

meta_op: Define a class to identify null pointer expressions, including both literal '0' values and NULL macro instances.
Identify expressions with type conversions between different types, excluding void pointer conversions and array-to-pointer decay.
meta_impl:
from Expr e, Type t1, Type t2
where
  t1 = e.getUnspecifiedType() and
  t2 = e.getFullyConverted().getUnspecifiedType() and
  t1 != t2 and
  (t1 instanceof PointerType or t2 instanceof PointerType) and
  not (t2 instanceof VoidPointerType and t1 instanceof PointerType) and
  not t2 instanceof BoolType and
  not e instanceof NullPointer and
  not t1.(ArrayType).getBaseType() = t2.(PointerType).getBaseType()

meta_op: Report illegal pointer casts with a diagnostic message showing both source and target types, enforcing AV Rule 182.
meta_impl:
select e,
  "AV Rule 182: illegal cast from type " + t1.toString() + " to type " + t2.toString() +
    ". Casting to or from pointers shall not be used."

meta_op: Define a predicate to identify necessary floating-point to integer conversions, which is initially empty to be extended as needed.
meta_impl: predicate necessaryFloatToIntConversion(Expr e) { none() }

meta_op: Define a predicate to identify problematic floating-point to integer conversions, excluding necessary conversions and ensuring the source is from the codebase.
Select expressions that represent problematic floating-point to integer conversions, ensuring only the outermost conversions are reported and excluding parenthesized expressions.
meta_impl:
from Expr e
where
  badConversion(e) and
  not badConversion(e.getParent()) and
  not e instanceof ParenthesisExpr

meta_op: Report the problematic conversion with a diagnostic message referencing AV Rule 184.
meta_impl: select e, "AV Rule 184: Floating point numbers shall not be converted to integers."

meta_op: Define a class for integer constant expressions that have known values and are of integral type.
Define a class for implicit conversions and provide methods to get source and target types and their properties.
Define a class for lossy implicit conversions by checking various conditions that may lead to information loss.
Select lossy implicit conversions that do not target boolean type and report them with a warning message.
meta_impl:
from LossyImplicitConversion lic
where
  // Conversions to bool are always fine
  not lic.getUnderlyingTargetType() instanceof BoolType
select lic,
  "AV Rule 180: implicit conversion from " + lic.getUnderlyingSourceType().toString() + " to " +
    lic.getUnderlyingTargetType().toString() + " may lose information."

meta_op: Identify all C-style casts in the code that originate from source files and are not C++ style casts (const_cast, reinterpret_cast, or static_cast).
meta_impl:
from Cast c
where
  c.fromSource() and
  c.getFile() instanceof CppFile and
  not c instanceof ConstCast and
  not c instanceof ReinterpretCast and
  not c instanceof StaticCast

meta_op: Select the root cause of the C-style cast and provide a diagnostic message recommending the use of C++ style casts instead.
meta_impl:
select c.findRootCause(),
  "AV Rule 184: C++ style casts shall be used instead of the traditional C-style casts."

meta_op: Identify source-level expressions that involve pointer conversions.
meta_impl:
from Expr e
where
  e.fromSource()

meta_op: Determine the declared type of the pointer being converted, focusing on its base type.
meta_impl: declared = e.getUnderlyingType().(PointerType).getBaseType().getUnderlyingType()

meta_op: Determine the actual type the pointer is being converted to, focusing on its base type.
meta_impl: converted = e.getActualType().(PointerType).getBaseType().getUnderlyingType()

meta_op: Find all derived classes (sub) of the converted type through any number of base class relationships.
meta_impl: converted.getABaseClass*() = sub

meta_op: Check if any derived class (sub) has a virtual base class (sup).
meta_impl: sub.hasVirtualBaseClass(sup)

meta_op: Verify that the virtual base class (sup) is transitively a base class of the declared type.
meta_impl: sup.getABaseClass*() = declared

meta_op: Report the expression that violates AV Rule 179 by converting a pointer to a virtual base class to a derived class pointer.
meta_impl:
select e,
  "AV Rule 179: A pointer to a virtual base class shall not be converted to a pointer to a derived class."

meta_op: Identify expressions that originate from source code and have an associated explicit conversion.
meta_impl:
from Expr e, Conversion c
where
  e.fromSource() and
  c = e.getConversion()

meta_op: Filter out conversions that are either parentheses expressions or compiler-generated.
meta_impl:
not c instanceof ParenthesisExpr and
  not c.isCompilerGenerated()

meta_op: Check if the underlying type of the conversion matches the underlying type of the expression, indicating redundancy.
meta_impl: c.getUnderlyingType() = e.getUnderlyingType()

meta_op: Select the root cause of the redundant conversion and emit a warning about AV Rule 181 violation.
meta_impl: select c.findRootCause(), "AV Rule 181: Redundant explicit casts will not be used."

meta_op: Count the number of set locations by evaluating the staged points-to predicate for unresolved elements.
meta_impl: select count(int set, Element location | setlocations(set, unresolveElement(location)))

meta_op: Count the number of points-to sets by evaluating the staged points-to predicate for unresolved elements.
meta_impl: count(int set, Element element | pointstosets(set, unresolveElement(element)))

meta_op: Define a custom PointsToExpr class that specifically targets field access expressions.
Identify a specific function by name and find all field accesses within it that target a field named 'pfFunction'.
meta_impl:
from Function outer, FieldAccessPT fa
where
  outer.hasName("rtLnDeliverableMayContainDividends") and
  fa.(FieldAccess).getTarget().hasName("pfFunction") and
  fa.getEnclosingFunction() = outer

meta_op: Select the identified field accesses and retrieve their points-to information for debugging purposes.
meta_impl: select fa, fa.pointsTo()

meta_op: Define functions and their argument positions that are considered input sources.
meta_impl:
predicate inputArgument(string function, int arg) {
  function = "read" and arg = 1
  or
  function = "fread" and arg = 0
  or
  function = "fgets" and arg = 0
}

meta_op: Identify expressions that are input buffers by checking if they are arguments to input functions.
Define a class for input buffer expressions that extends PointsToExpr and implements the interesting predicate.
Define functions and their argument positions that are considered format string sinks.
meta_impl:
predicate formatArgument(string function, int i) {
  function = "printf" and i = 0
  or
  function = "fprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "sprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "snprintf" and i = 2
  or
  function = "d_printf" and i = 0
  or
  function = "talloc_asprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "fstr_sprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "talloc_asprintf_append" and i = 1
  or
  function = "d_fprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "asprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "talloc_asprintf_append_buffer" and i = 1
  or
  function = "fdprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "d_vfprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "smb_xvasprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "asprintf_strupper_m" and i = 1
  or
  function = "talloc_asprintf_strupper_m" and i = 1
  or
  function = "sprintf_append" and i = 4
  or
  function = "x_vfprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "x_fprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "vasprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "ldb_asprintf_errstring" and i = 1
  or
  function = "talloc_vasprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "talloc_vasprintf" and i = 1
  or
  function = "fprintf_file" and i = 1
  or
  function = "vsnprintf" and i = 2
  or
  function = "talloc_vasprintf_append" and i = 1
  or
  function = "__talloc_vaslenprintf_append" and i = 2
  or
  function = "talloc_vasprintf_append_buffer" and i = 1
  or
  function = "fprintf_attr" and i = 2
  or
  function = "vprintf" and i = 0
  or
  function = "vsprintf" and i = 1
}

meta_op: Identify expressions that are format buffers by checking if they are arguments to format functions.
Define a class for format buffer expressions that extends PointsToExpr and implements the interesting predicate.
Define potential taint violations where an input buffer points to the same location as a format buffer, excluding certain safe cases.
Select and report potential taint violations between input buffers and format buffers.
meta_impl:
from InputBuffer source, FormatBuffer dest
where potentialViolation(source, dest)
select dest.getFile() as file, dest as formatString

meta_op: Define a predicate to determine if a points-to set has incoming flow edges and count them, excluding self-edges.
Count the total number of points-to sets that have any incoming flow edges.
meta_impl: count(int set | inc(set, _)) as total

meta_op: Count the number of points-to sets that have zero incoming flow edges.
meta_impl: count(int set | inc(set, 0)) as nullary

meta_op: Count the number of points-to sets that have exactly one incoming flow edge.
meta_impl: count(int set | inc(set, 1)) as unary

meta_op: Calculate the number of points-to sets with more than one incoming flow edge by subtracting nullary and unary counts from the total.
meta_impl: total - nullary - unary as rest
